_**User (2025-07-27 13:43:38Z)**_

i got teh feeling there is a lot of complexity in data wrangling in the service_layer. 
i currenty have a look at get_special_matches.
I want to delegate tathe to the data adapter:
lets implent the following mehod and the necessary call stack: 

team_matches = self.server.get_matches(team=team_name, season=None, league=None, opponent_tean_name=None)

the method shall return data with the cols:
league, seasons, week, team_name, round_number, score (accumulated team score), oppponent_team_name, opponent_score (accumulated opponent team score) 

all of this data is in the original DB, only the opponent_team_score is missing.
the method should add the respective col and fill in the data for teh seleceted event (combo of season, league, week, round_number). 
so we end up with a df taht contains both team and opponent score we can easily use for min max scores as well as margins

 

---

I'll help you implement the `get_matches` method in the data adapter and the necessary call stack. Let me first explore the current structure to understand the data flow and existing implementations.

```json
        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n    def get_special_matches(self, team_name: str=None):\r\n        \"\"\"Returns special matches for a team\"\"\"\r\n        if team_name is None:\r\n            return {}\r\n        \r\n        team_matches = self.server.get_matches(team=team_name, season=None, league=None, opponent_tean_name=None)\r\n\r\n        seasons = self.server.get_seasons(team_name=team_name)\r\n        if seasons is None:\r\n            return {}\r\n        \r\n        special_matches = {\r\n            \"highest_scores\": [],\r\n            \"lowest_scores\": [],\r\n            \"biggest_win_margin\": [],\r\n            \"biggest_loss_margin\": []\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        for season in seasons:\r\n            league_name = self.server.get_leagues(team_name=team_name, season=season)\r\n            if isinstance(league_name, list):\r\n                league_name = league_name[0]\r\n            else:\r\n                continue\r\n                \r\n            # Get team statistics for this season\r\n            team_stats = self.get_team_statistics(team_name, season)\r\n            if not team_stats:\r\n                continue",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class Server:\r\n    # fetches basic dataframes from data adapter\r\n    # converts basic dataframes to aggregated dataframes\r\n    # forwards aggregated dataframes to app\n\r\n\r\n    ...\n\r\n\r\n    def get_games_for_player(self, player_name: str) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        \"\"\"Get all games for a player\"\"\"\r\n        player_filters = {\r\n            Columns.player_name: {\r\n                'value': player_name,\r\n                'operator': 'eq'\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        columns = [Columns.player_id, Columns.player_name, Columns.date, Columns.week, Columns.season, Columns.league_name, Columns.team_name, Columns.score, Columns.points]\r\n        return self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(columns=columns, filters=player_filters)\r\n\r\n    def get_special_matches(self, team_name: str=None, amount: int=3):\r\n\r\n        columns = [Columns.week, Columns.season, Columns.league_name, Columns.round_number, Columns.team_name, Columns.score, Columns.points, Columns.team_name_opponent]\r\n        filters_team = {\r\n            Columns.team_name: {\r\n                'value': team_name,\r\n                'operator': 'eq'\r\n            },\r\n            Columns.computed_data: {\r\n                'value': True,\r\n                'operator': 'eq'\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        filters_opponent = {\r\n            Columns.team_name_opponent: {\r\n                'value': team_name,\r\n                'operator': 'eq'\r\n            },\r\n            Columns.computed_data: {\r\n                'value': True,\r\n                'operator': 'eq'\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        data_team = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(columns=columns, filters=filters_team)\r\n        data_opponent = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(columns=columns, filters=filters_opponent)\r\n\r\n        # merge data_team and data_opponent\r\n        data_combined = pd.concat([data_team, data_opponent])\r\n        data_matches = data_combined.groupby([Columns.season, Columns.week, Columns.round_number])\r\n        \r\n        \r\n        # determin win margins\r\n        data_team[ColumnsExtra.margin] = int(0)",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n    def get_games_for_player(self, player_name: str) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        \"\"\"Get all games for a player\"\"\"\r\n        player_filters = {\r\n            Columns.player_name: {\r\n                'value': player_name,\r\n                'operator': 'eq'\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        columns = [Columns.player_id, Columns.player_name, Columns.date, Columns.week, Columns.season, Columns.league_name, Columns.team_name, Columns.score, Columns.points]\r\n        return self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(columns=columns, filters=player_filters)\r\n\r\n    def get_special_matches(self, team_name: str=None, amount: int=3):\r\n\r\n        columns = [Columns.week, Columns.season, Columns.league_name, Columns.round_number, Columns.team_name, Columns.score, Columns.points, Columns.team_name_opponent]\r\n        filters_team = {\r\n            Columns.team_name: {\r\n                'value': team_name,\r\n                'operator': 'eq'\r\n            },\r\n            Columns.computed_data: {\r\n                'value': True,\r\n                'operator': 'eq'\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        filters_opponent = {\r\n            Columns.team_name_opponent: {\r\n                'value': team_name,\r\n                'operator': 'eq'\r\n            },\r\n            Columns.computed_data: {\r\n                'value': True,\r\n                'operator': 'eq'\r\n            }\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        data_team = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(columns=columns, filters=filters_team)\r\n        data_opponent = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(columns=columns, filters=filters_opponent)\r\n\r\n        # merge data_team and data_opponent\r\n        data_combined = pd.concat([data_team, data_opponent])\r\n        data_matches = data_combined.groupby([Columns.season, Columns.week, Columns.round_number])\r\n        \r\n        \r\n        # determin win margins\r\n        data_team[ColumnsExtra.margin] = int(0)",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class Server:\r\n    # fetches basic dataframes from data adapter\r\n    # converts basic dataframes to aggregated dataframes\r\n    # forwards aggregated dataframes to app\n    ...\n    def get_special_matches(self, team_name: str=None, amount: int=3):\n\r\n        ...\n\r\n        for (season, week, round_number), group in data_matches:\r\n            #print(name)\r\n            #print(group)\r\n\r\n\r\n            team_score = group[group[Columns.team_name] == team_name][Columns.score].iloc[0]\r\n            \r\n            # Get the score when the team was playing as opponent\r\n            opponent_score = group[group[Columns.team_name_opponent] == team_name][Columns.score].iloc[0]\r\n\r\n            data_team.loc[(data_team[Columns.season] == season) \u0026 (data_team[Columns.week] == week) \u0026 (data_team[Columns.round_number] == round_number), ColumnsExtra.margin] = team_score - opponent_score\r\n\r\n        # highest scores\r\n        highest_scores = data_team.sort_values(by=Columns.score, ascending=False).head(amount)\r\n\r\n        # lowest scores\r\n        lowest_scores = data_team.sort_values(by=Columns.score, ascending=True).head(amount)\r\n\r\n        # biggest win margin\r\n        biggest_win_margin = data_team.sort_values(by=ColumnsExtra.margin, ascending=False).head(amount)\r\n\r\n        # biggest loss margin\r\n        biggest_loss_margin = data_team.sort_values(by=ColumnsExtra.margin, ascending=True).head(amount)\r\n\r\n        # highest scoring match ( own score + opponent score )\r\n        #highest_scoring_match = data_team.sort_values(by=2 * Columns.score - ColumnsExtra.margin, ascending=False).head(amount)\r\n\r\n        # lowest scoring match ( own score + opponent score )\r\n        #lowest_scoring_match = data_team.sort_values(by=2 * Columns.score - ColumnsExtra.margin, ascending=True).head(amount)\r\n\r\n        # compose dictionary\r\n        special_matches = {\r\n            'highest_scores': highest_scores.to_dict(orient='records'),\r\n            'lowest_scores': lowest_scores.to_dict(orient='records'),\r\n            'biggest_win_margin': biggest_win_margin.to_dict(orient='records'),\r\n            'biggest_loss_margin': biggest_loss_margin.to_dict(orient='records')\r\n            #'highest_scoring_match': highest_scoring_match.to_dict(orient='records'),\r\n            #'lowest_scoring_match': lowest_scoring_match.to_dict(orient='records'),\r\n        }",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class TeamService:\r\n    # fetches dataframes from server\r\n    # converts dataframes to dicts\r\n    # jsonifies dicts   \r\n    # forwards JSON dict to app routes\n    ...\n    def get_special_matches(self, team_name: str=None):\n\r\n        ...\n\r\n        try:\r\n            # Get team data for the season\r\n            team_filters = {\r\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.team_name: {'value': team_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            team_data = self.server.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=team_filters)\r\n            \r\n            if team_data.empty:\r\n                return {\"error\": \"No team data found\"}\r\n            \r\n            # Sort by week to get chronological order\r\n            team_data = team_data.sort_values(Columns.week)\r\n            \r\n            current_streak = 0\r\n            current_streak_type = None  # \"W\" or \"L\"\r\n            max_win_streak = 0\r\n            max_loss_streak = 0\r\n            current_win_streak = 0\r\n            current_loss_streak = 0\r\n            streak_history = []",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "class TeamService:\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "    # fetches dataframes from server\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "    # converts dataframes to dicts\r"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "    # jsonifies dicts   \r"

        "contents": "class TeamService:\r\n    # fetches dataframes from server\r\n    # converts dataframes to dicts\r\n    # jsonifies dicts   \r\n    # forwards JSON dict to app routes\n    ...\n    def get_special_matches(self, team_name: str=None):\n\r\n                \r\n            ...\n\r\n                \r\n            # Get weekly performances\r\n            for week, week_perf in team_stats.weekly_performances.items():\r\n                # Get opponent data for this week\r\n                opponent_filters = {\r\n                    Columns.league_name: {'value': league_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                    Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                    Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                    Columns.team_name_opponent: {'value': team_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                    Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                opponent_data = self.server.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=opponent_filters)\r\n                if not opponent_data.empty:\r\n                    opponent_score = opponent_data[Columns.score].iloc[0]\r\n                    opponent_name = opponent_data[Columns.team_name].iloc[0]\r\n                    \r\n                    # Calculate win margin\r\n                    win_margin = week_perf.total_score - opponent_score\r\n                    \r\n                    match_info = {\r\n                        \"Season\": season,\r\n                        \"League\": league_name,\r\n                        \"Week\": int(week),\r\n                        \"Score\": float(week_perf.total_score),\r\n                        \"Opponent\": opponent_name,\r\n                        \"OpponentScore\": float(opponent_score),\r\n                        \"WinMargin\": float(win_margin)\r\n                    }\r\n                    \r\n                    # Add to appropriate lists\r\n                    special_matches[\"highest_scores\"].append(match_info)\r\n                    special_matches[\"lowest_scores\"].append(match_info)\r\n                    special_matches[\"biggest_win_margin\"].append(match_info)\r\n                    special_matches[\"biggest_loss_margin\"].append(match_info)",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class Server:\r\n    # fetches basic dataframes from data adapter\r\n    # converts basic dataframes to aggregated dataframes\r\n    # forwards aggregated dataframes to app\n    ...\n    def get_special_matches(self, team_name: str=None, amount: int=3):\n\r\n\r\n        ...\n\r\n\r\n        \"\"\"Returns the special matches for a given team\"\"\"\r\n        return special_matches\r\n    \r\n    def get_latest_events(self, limit=5) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        \"\"\"Get the latest league events based on date.\"\"\"\r\n        print(\"server.get_latest_events\")\r\n\r\n        # Get unique combinations of season, league, week, and date\r\n        data =  self.data_adapter.get_latest_events(limit)\r\n        print(data)\r\n        return data\r\n\r\n    def get_league_season(self, league_name: str, season: str) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        High level database query.\r\n        Composes a table that represents a league's results in a given season.\r\n\r\n        Args:\r\n            league_name (str): The name of the league.\r\n            season (str): The season.\r\n\r\n        Returns:\r\n            pd.DataFrame: The league standings for the given season.\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        columns_to_fetch = [Columns.team_name, Columns.points, Columns.score, Columns.week]\r\n        \r\n        # Use the new filter format\r\n        filters = {\r\n            Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.league_name: {'value': league_name, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        league_season_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(columns=columns_to_fetch, filters=filters)\r\n        \r\n        return league_season_data",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n        \"\"\"Returns the special matches for a given team\"\"\"\r\n        return special_matches\r\n    \r\n    def get_latest_events(self, limit=5) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        \"\"\"Get the latest league events based on date.\"\"\"\r\n        print(\"server.get_latest_events\")\r\n\r\n        # Get unique combinations of season, league, week, and date\r\n        data =  self.data_adapter.get_latest_events(limit)\r\n        print(data)\r\n        return data\r\n\r\n    def get_league_season(self, league_name: str, season: str) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        High level database query.\r\n        Composes a table that represents a league's results in a given season.\r\n\r\n        Args:\r\n            league_name (str): The name of the league.\r\n            season (str): The season.\r\n\r\n        Returns:\r\n            pd.DataFrame: The league standings for the given season.\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        columns_to_fetch = [Columns.team_name, Columns.points, Columns.score, Columns.week]\r\n        \r\n        # Use the new filter format\r\n        filters = {\r\n            Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n            Columns.league_name: {'value': league_name, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        league_season_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(columns=columns_to_fetch, filters=filters)\r\n        \r\n        return league_season_data",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class TeamService:\r\n    # fetches dataframes from server\r\n    # converts dataframes to dicts\r\n    # jsonifies dicts   \r\n    # forwards JSON dict to app routes\n    ...\n    def get_special_matches(self, team_name: str=None):\n\r\n        ...\n\r\n        try:\r\n            # Get team data for the season\r\n            team_filters = {\r\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.team_name: {'value': team_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            team_data = self.server.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=team_filters)\r\n            \r\n            if team_data.empty:\r\n                return {\"error\": \"No team data found\"}\r\n            \r\n            scores = team_data[Columns.score].tolist()\r\n            \r\n            if len(scores) \u003c 2:\r\n                return {\"error\": \"Insufficient data for consistency analysis\"}\r\n            \r\n            # Calculate basic statistics\r\n            mean_score = sum(scores) / len(scores)\r\n            variance = sum((x - mean_score) ** 2 for x in scores) / len(scores)\r\n            std_dev = variance ** 0.5\r\n            coefficient_of_variation = (std_dev / mean_score) * 100 if mean_score \u003e 0 else 0\r\n            \r\n            # Calculate range\r\n            min_score = min(scores)\r\n            max_score = max(scores)\r\n            score_range = max_score - min_score\r\n            \r\n            # Calculate quartiles\r\n            sorted_scores = sorted(scores)\r\n            n = len(sorted_scores)\r\n            q1 = sorted_scores[n // 4] if n \u003e= 4 else sorted_scores[0]\r\n            q3 = sorted_scores[3 * n // 4] if n \u003e= 4 else sorted_scores[-1]\r\n            iqr = q3 - q1",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "\r\n        try:\r\n            # Get team data for the season\r\n            team_filters = {\r\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.team_name: {'value': team_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            team_data = self.server.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=team_filters)\r\n            \r\n            if team_data.empty:\r\n                return {\"error\": \"No team data found\"}\r\n            \r\n            scores = team_data[Columns.score].tolist()\r\n            \r\n            if len(scores) \u003c 2:\r\n                return {\"error\": \"Insufficient data for consistency analysis\"}\r\n            \r\n            # Calculate basic statistics\r\n            mean_score = sum(scores) / len(scores)\r\n            variance = sum((x - mean_score) ** 2 for x in scores) / len(scores)\r\n            std_dev = variance ** 0.5\r\n            coefficient_of_variation = (std_dev / mean_score) * 100 if mean_score \u003e 0 else 0\r\n            \r\n            # Calculate range\r\n            min_score = min(scores)\r\n            max_score = max(scores)\r\n            score_range = max_score - min_score\r\n            \r\n            # Calculate quartiles\r\n            sorted_scores = sorted(scores)\r\n            n = len(sorted_scores)\r\n            q1 = sorted_scores[n // 4] if n \u003e= 4 else sorted_scores[0]\r\n            q3 = sorted_scores[3 * n // 4] if n \u003e= 4 else sorted_scores[-1]\r\n            iqr = q3 - q1",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class TeamService:\r\n    # fetches dataframes from server\r\n    # converts dataframes to dicts\r\n    # jsonifies dicts   \r\n    # forwards JSON dict to app routes\n    ...\n    def get_special_matches(self, team_name: str=None):\n\r\n                ...\n\r\n                week_team_data = team_data[team_data[Columns.week] == week]\r\n                if week_team_data.empty:\r\n                    continue\r\n                \r\n                team_score = week_team_data[Columns.score].iloc[0]\r\n                \r\n                # Get opponent data for this week\r\n                opponent_filters = {\r\n                    Columns.league_name: {'value': league_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                    Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                    Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                    Columns.team_name_opponent: {'value': team_name, 'operator': 'eq'},\r\n                    Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\r\n                }\r\n                \r\n                opponent_data = self.server.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=opponent_filters)\r\n                if opponent_data.empty:\r\n                    continue\r\n                \r\n                opponent_score = opponent_data[Columns.score].iloc[0]\r\n                opponent_name = opponent_data[Columns.team_name].iloc[0]\r\n                \r\n                margin = abs(team_score - opponent_score)\r\n                total_games += 1\r\n                \r\n                # Check if it's a clutch game (margin \u003c 10)\r\n                if margin \u003c 10:\r\n                    is_win = team_score \u003e opponent_score\r\n                    clutch_games.append({\r\n                        \"week\": week,\r\n                        \"team_score\": float(team_score),\r\n                        \"opponent_score\": float(opponent_score),\r\n                        \"opponent\": opponent_name,\r\n                        \"margin\": float(margin),\r\n                        \"result\": \"W\" if is_win else \"L\"\r\n                    })\r\n                    \r\n                    if is_win:\r\n                        clutch_wins += 1\r\n                    else:\r\n                        clutch_losses += 1",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class TeamService:\r\n    # fetches dataframes from server\r\n    # converts dataframes to dicts\r\n    # jsonifies dicts   \r\n    # forwards JSON dict to app routes\n    ...\n    def get_special_matches(self, team_name: str=None):\n\r\n        \r\n        ...\n\r\n        \r\n        # Sort and take top 5 for each category\r\n        special_matches[\"highest_scores\"] = sorted(special_matches[\"highest_scores\"], key=lambda x: x[\"Score\"], reverse=True)[:5]\r\n        special_matches[\"lowest_scores\"] = sorted(special_matches[\"lowest_scores\"], key=lambda x: x[\"Score\"])[:5]\r\n        special_matches[\"biggest_win_margin\"] = sorted(special_matches[\"biggest_win_margin\"], key=lambda x: x[\"WinMargin\"], reverse=True)[:5]\r\n        special_matches[\"biggest_loss_margin\"] = sorted(special_matches[\"biggest_loss_margin\"], key=lambda x: x[\"WinMargin\"])[:5]\r\n        \r\n        return special_matches\r\n\r\n    def get_clutch_performance(self, team_name: str, league_name: str, season: str) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        Analyze clutch performance - games decided by close margins (\u003c10 points)\r\n        \r\n        Args:\r\n            team_name: Name of the team\r\n            league_name: Name of the league\r\n            season: Season identifier\r\n            \r\n        Returns:\r\n            Dictionary with clutch performance data\r\n        \"\"\"",

        "contents": "class TeamService:\r\n    # fetches dataframes from server\r\n    # converts dataframes to dicts\r\n    # jsonifies dicts   \r\n    # forwards JSON dict to app routes\n    ...\n    def get_special_matches(self, team_name: str=None):\n\r\n                \r\n                ...\n\r\n                \r\n                if is_win:\r\n                    if current_streak_type == \"W\":\r\n                        current_streak += 1\r\n                        current_win_streak += 1\r\n                    else:\r\n                        # End previous streak\r\n                        if current_streak \u003e 0:\r\n                            streak_history.append({\r\n                                \"type\": current_streak_type,\r\n                                \"length\": current_streak,\r\n                                \"start_week\": week - current_streak,\r\n                                \"end_week\": week - 1\r\n                            })\r\n                        \r\n                        # Start new win streak\r\n                        current_streak = 1\r\n                        current_streak_type = \"W\"\r\n                        current_win_streak = 1\r\n                        current_loss_streak = 0\r\n                else:\r\n                    if current_streak_type == \"L\":\r\n                        current_streak += 1\r\n                        current_loss_streak += 1\r\n                    else:\r\n                        # End previous streak\r\n                        if current_streak \u003e 0:\r\n                            streak_history.append({\r\n                                \"type\": current_streak_type,\r\n                                \"length\": current_streak,\r\n                                \"start_week\": week - current_streak,\r\n                                \"end_week\": week - 1\r\n                            })\r\n                        \r\n                        # Start new loss streak\r\n                        current_streak = 1\r\n                        current_streak_type = \"L\"\r\n                        current_loss_streak = 1\r\n                        current_win_streak = 0\r\n                \r\n                # Update max streaks\r\n                max_win_streak = max(max_win_streak, current_win_streak)\r\n                max_loss_streak = max(max_loss_streak, current_loss_streak)",

        "contents": "class TeamService:\r\n    # fetches dataframes from server\r\n    # converts dataframes to dicts\r\n    # jsonifies dicts   \r\n    # forwards JSON dict to app routes\n    ...\n    def get_special_matches(self, team_name: str=None):\n\r\n            \r\n            ...\n\r\n            \r\n            return {\r\n                \"mean_score\": round(mean_score, 1),\r\n                \"std_deviation\": round(std_dev, 1),\r\n                \"coefficient_of_variation\": round(coefficient_of_variation, 1),\r\n                \"score_range\": score_range,\r\n                \"min_score\": min_score,\r\n                \"max_score\": max_score,\r\n                \"q1\": q1,\r\n                \"q3\": q3,\r\n                \"iqr\": iqr,\r\n                \"consistency_rating\": self._calculate_consistency_rating(coefficient_of_variation)\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n        except Exception as e:\r\n            print(f\"Error in get_consistency_metrics: {str(e)}\")\r\n            return {\"error\": str(e)}\r\n\r\n    def get_streak_analysis(self, team_name: str, league_name: str, season: str) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        Analyze winning and losing streaks\r\n        \r\n        Args:\r\n            team_name: Name of the team\r\n            league_name: Name of the league\r\n            season: Season identifier\r\n            \r\n        Returns:\r\n            Dictionary with streak analysis\r\n        \"\"\"",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n        function displaySpecialMatches(data) {\r\n            // Helper function to format event string\r\n            const formatEvent = (match) =\u003e &#96;${match.Season} ${match.League} W${match.Week}&#96;;\r\n\r\n            // Populate highest scores\r\n            document.getElementById('highestScoresBody').innerHTML = data.highest_scores.map(match =\u003e &#96;\r\n                \u003ctr\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Score}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${formatEvent(match)}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Opponent}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                \u003c/tr\u003e\r\n            &#96;).join('');\r\n\r\n            // Populate lowest scores\r\n            document.getElementById('lowestScoresBody').innerHTML = data.lowest_scores.map(match =\u003e &#96;\r\n                \u003ctr\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Score}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${formatEvent(match)}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Opponent}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                \u003c/tr\u003e\r\n            &#96;).join('');\r\n\r\n            // Populate biggest wins\r\n            document.getElementById('biggestWinsBody').innerHTML = data.biggest_win_margin.map(match =\u003e &#96;\r\n                \u003ctr\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e+${match.WinMargin}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Score} : ${match.Score - match.WinMargin}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${formatEvent(match)}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Opponent}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                \u003c/tr\u003e\r\n            &#96;).join('');\r\n\r\n            // Populate biggest losses\r\n            document.getElementById('biggestLossesBody').innerHTML = data.biggest_loss_margin.map(match =\u003e &#96;\r\n                \u003ctr\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.WinMargin}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Score} : ${match.Score - match.WinMargin}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${formatEvent(match)}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Opponent}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                \u003c/tr\u003e\r\n            &#96;).join('');\r\n        }\r\n    \u003c/script\u003e\r\n{% endblock %} ",

        "originalContents": "\r\n        function displaySpecialMatches(data) {\r\n            // Helper function to format event string\r\n            const formatEvent = (match) =\u003e &#96;${match.Season} ${match.League} W${match.Week}&#96;;\r\n\r\n            // Populate highest scores\r\n            document.getElementById('highestScoresBody').innerHTML = data.highest_scores.map(match =\u003e &#96;\r\n                \u003ctr\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Score}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${formatEvent(match)}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Opponent}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                \u003c/tr\u003e\r\n            &#96;).join('');\r\n\r\n            // Populate lowest scores\r\n            document.getElementById('lowestScoresBody').innerHTML = data.lowest_scores.map(match =\u003e &#96;\r\n                \u003ctr\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Score}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${formatEvent(match)}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Opponent}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                \u003c/tr\u003e\r\n            &#96;).join('');\r\n\r\n            // Populate biggest wins\r\n            document.getElementById('biggestWinsBody').innerHTML = data.biggest_win_margin.map(match =\u003e &#96;\r\n                \u003ctr\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e+${match.WinMargin}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Score} : ${match.Score - match.WinMargin}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${formatEvent(match)}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Opponent}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                \u003c/tr\u003e\r\n            &#96;).join('');\r\n\r\n            // Populate biggest losses\r\n            document.getElementById('biggestLossesBody').innerHTML = data.biggest_loss_margin.map(match =\u003e &#96;\r\n                \u003ctr\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.WinMargin}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Score} : ${match.Score - match.WinMargin}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${formatEvent(match)}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                    \u003ctd\u003e${match.Opponent}\u003c/td\u003e\r\n                \u003c/tr\u003e\r\n            &#96;).join('');\r\n        }\r\n    \u003c/script\u003e\r\n{% endblock %} ",

        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n\n\n    ...\n\n\n    def get_team_week_head_to_head_table_data(self, league: str, season: str, team: str, week: int, view_mode: str = 'own_team') -\u003e TableData:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get head-to-head comparison table data for a team vs their opponents in a specific week.\n        view_mode: 'own_team' (default), 'opponents', 'full'\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Get all matches for the team in this week\n            team_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.team_name: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }\n            team_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=team_filters)\n            if team_data.empty:\n                return TableData(\n                    columns=[],\n                    data=[],\n                    title=f\"No data available for {team} - Week {week}\"\n                )\n            \n            # Get all matches for the opponent teams in this week\n            opponent_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.team_name_opponent: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }\n            opponent_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=opponent_filters)\n            \n            # Get unique round numbers (matches)\n            all_rounds = sorted(set(team_data[Columns.round_number].unique()) | set(opponent_data[Columns.round_number].unique()))\n\n            # --- Determine which players to show ---\n            show_own = view_mode in ('own_team', 'full')",

            "lineNumber": 1590,
            "text": "            show_own = view_mode in ('own_team', 'full')"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n    def get_team_week_head_to_head_table_data(self, league: str, season: str, team: str, week: int, view_mode: str = 'own_team') -\u003e TableData:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get head-to-head comparison table data for a team vs their opponents in a specific week.\n        view_mode: 'own_team' (default), 'opponents', 'full'\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Get all matches for the team in this week\n            team_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.team_name: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }\n            team_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=team_filters)\n            if team_data.empty:\n                return TableData(\n                    columns=[],\n                    data=[],\n                    title=f\"No data available for {team} - Week {week}\"\n                )\n            \n            # Get all matches for the opponent teams in this week\n            opponent_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.team_name_opponent: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }\n            opponent_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=opponent_filters)\n            \n            # Get unique round numbers (matches)\n            all_rounds = sorted(set(team_data[Columns.round_number].unique()) | set(opponent_data[Columns.round_number].unique()))\n\n            # --- Determine which players to show ---\n            show_own = view_mode in ('own_team', 'full')",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 57,
            "line": 1590
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 37,
            "line": 1552
          }

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class DataAdapterPandas(DataAdapter):\n    ...\n    def get_filtered_data__deprecated(self, columns: List[Columns]=None, filters_eq: dict=None, filters_lt: dict=None, filters_gt: dict=None, print_debug: bool=False) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\n\r\n        \r\n        ...\n\r\n        \r\n        try:\r\n            # Get unique weeks, sort them, and convert to list\r\n            weeks = sorted(filtered_df[Columns.week].unique().tolist())\r\n            # Filter out None/NaN values if any\r\n            weeks = [week for week in weeks if week is not None]\r\n            #print(f\"Available weeks: {weeks}\")  # Debug output\r\n            return weeks\r\n        except Exception as e:\r\n            print(f\"Error getting weeks: {str(e)}\")\r\n            return []\r\n    \r\n    def get_league_match_day(self, league_name: str, season: str, week: int) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        return self.df[(self.df[Columns.league_name] == league_name) \u0026 (self.df[Columns.season] == season) \u0026 (self.df[Columns.week] == week)]\r\n    \r\n    def get_league_season(self, league_name: str, season: str, week: int=None) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        if week is None:\r\n            return self.df[self.df[Columns.league_name] == league_name][self.df[Columns.season] == season]\r\n        else:\r\n            return self.df[self.df[Columns.league_name] == league_name][self.df[Columns.season] == season][self.df[Columns.week] \u003c= week]\r\n\r\n    def get_latest_events(self, limit=5):\r\n        \"\"\"Get the latest league events based on date.\"\"\"\r\n        # Get unique combinations of season, league, week, and date\r\n        events = self.df[[Columns.season, Columns.league_name, Columns.week, Columns.date]].drop_duplicates()\r\n\r\n        # Sort by date in descending order and get the latest events\r\n        latest_events = events.sort_values(by=Columns.date, ascending=False).head(limit)\r\n\r\n        return latest_events",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "\r\n        \r\n        try:\r\n            # Get unique weeks, sort them, and convert to list\r\n            weeks = sorted(filtered_df[Columns.week].unique().tolist())\r\n            # Filter out None/NaN values if any\r\n            weeks = [week for week in weeks if week is not None]\r\n            #print(f\"Available weeks: {weeks}\")  # Debug output\r\n            return weeks\r\n        except Exception as e:\r\n            print(f\"Error getting weeks: {str(e)}\")\r\n            return []\r\n    \r\n    def get_league_match_day(self, league_name: str, season: str, week: int) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        return self.df[(self.df[Columns.league_name] == league_name) \u0026 (self.df[Columns.season] == season) \u0026 (self.df[Columns.week] == week)]\r\n    \r\n    def get_league_season(self, league_name: str, season: str, week: int=None) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        if week is None:\r\n            return self.df[self.df[Columns.league_name] == league_name][self.df[Columns.season] == season]\r\n        else:\r\n            return self.df[self.df[Columns.league_name] == league_name][self.df[Columns.season] == season][self.df[Columns.week] \u003c= week]\r\n\r\n    def get_latest_events(self, limit=5):\r\n        \"\"\"Get the latest league events based on date.\"\"\"\r\n        # Get unique combinations of season, league, week, and date\r\n        events = self.df[[Columns.season, Columns.league_name, Columns.week, Columns.date]].drop_duplicates()\r\n\r\n        # Sort by date in descending order and get the latest events\r\n        latest_events = events.sort_values(by=Columns.date, ascending=False).head(limit)\r\n\r\n        return latest_events",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional\nimport datetime\nimport pandas as pd\n\n\nfrom data_access.adapters.data_adapter_factory import DataAdapterFactory, DataAdapterSelector\nfrom data_access.models.league_models import LeagueQuery, TeamSeasonPerformance, LeagueStandings, TeamWeeklyPerformance\nfrom app.services.i18n_service import i18n_service\nfrom app.models.table_data import TableData, ColumnGroup, Column, PlotData, TileData\nfrom app.services.statistics_service import StatisticsService\nfrom app.models.statistics_models import LeagueStatistics, LeagueResults\nfrom app.models.series_data import SeriesData\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns\nfrom itertools import accumulate\n# from data_access.series_data import calculate_series_data, get_player_series_data, get_team_series_data\n\n\nclass LeagueService:\n    def __init__(self, adapter_type=DataAdapterSelector.PANDAS):\n        self.adapter = DataAdapterFactory.create_adapter(adapter_type)\n        self.stats_service = StatisticsService()\n\n    def get_available_weeks(self, season: str, league: str) -\u003e List[int]:\n        \"\"\"Get available weeks for a season and league\"\"\"\n        return self.adapter.get_weeks(season, league)\n    \n    def get_latest_week(self, season: str, league: str) -\u003e int:\n        \"\"\"Get the latest week number for a season and league\"\"\"\n        weeks = self.get_available_weeks(season, league)\n        return max(weeks) if weeks else 1\n    \n    def get_seasons(self) -\u003e List[str]:\n        \"\"\"Get all available seasons\"\"\"\n        return self.adapter.get_seasons()\n\n    def get_leagues(self) -\u003e List[str]:\n        \"\"\"Get all available leagues for a season\"\"\"\n        return self.adapter.get_leagues()",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

        "originalContents": "from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional\nimport datetime\nimport pandas as pd\n\n\nfrom data_access.adapters.data_adapter_factory import DataAdapterFactory, DataAdapterSelector\nfrom data_access.models.league_models import LeagueQuery, TeamSeasonPerformance, LeagueStandings, TeamWeeklyPerformance\nfrom app.services.i18n_service import i18n_service\nfrom app.models.table_data import TableData, ColumnGroup, Column, PlotData, TileData\nfrom app.services.statistics_service import StatisticsService\nfrom app.models.statistics_models import LeagueStatistics, LeagueResults\nfrom app.models.series_data import SeriesData\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns\nfrom itertools import accumulate\n# from data_access.series_data import calculate_series_data, get_player_series_data, get_team_series_data\n\n\nclass LeagueService:\n    def __init__(self, adapter_type=DataAdapterSelector.PANDAS):\n        self.adapter = DataAdapterFactory.create_adapter(adapter_type)\n        self.stats_service = StatisticsService()\n\n    def get_available_weeks(self, season: str, league: str) -\u003e List[int]:\n        \"\"\"Get available weeks for a season and league\"\"\"\n        return self.adapter.get_weeks(season, league)\n    \n    def get_latest_week(self, season: str, league: str) -\u003e int:\n        \"\"\"Get the latest week number for a season and league\"\"\"\n        weeks = self.get_available_weeks(season, league)\n        return max(weeks) if weeks else 1\n    \n    def get_seasons(self) -\u003e List[str]:\n        \"\"\"Get all available seasons\"\"\"\n        return self.adapter.get_seasons()\n\n    def get_leagues(self) -\u003e List[str]:\n        \"\"\"Get all available leagues for a season\"\"\"\n        return self.adapter.get_leagues()",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 42,
            "line": 38
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 1,
            "line": 1
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "app/services/league_service.py"
      },
      "score": 0.16015625
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n\n\n    ...\n\n\n    def get_league_table_simple(self, season: str, league: str, week: Optional[int] = None, include_history: bool = False) -\u003e TableData:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get a simplified league table with direct data query.\n        Can handle both single week and multiple weeks (history).\n        \n        Args:\n            season: The season identifier\n            league: The league name\n            week: The current week (if None, gets the latest)\n            include_history: If True, shows all weeks up to the selected week\n            \n        Returns:\n            TableData object with the league standings\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # If week is not specified, get the latest week\n            if week is None:\n                week = self.get_latest_week(season, league)\n            \n            # Direct query to get all data for this league and season (both individual and team points)\n            # Get individual player data (for scores and averages)\n            individual_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }\n            \n            individual_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=individual_filters)\n            \n            # Get team bonus data (for team points)\n            team_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }\n            \n            team_bonus_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=team_filters)\n            \n            # Use individual data for the main league data (scores and averages)\n            league_data = individual_data",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,

          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n    def get_league_table_simple(self, season: str, league: str, week: Optional[int] = None, include_history: bool = False) -\u003e TableData:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get a simplified league table with direct data query.\n        Can handle both single week and multiple weeks (history).\n        \n        Args:\n            season: The season identifier\n            league: The league name\n            week: The current week (if None, gets the latest)\n            include_history: If True, shows all weeks up to the selected week\n            \n        Returns:\n            TableData object with the league standings\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            # If week is not specified, get the latest week\n            if week is None:\n                week = self.get_latest_week(season, league)\n            \n            # Direct query to get all data for this league and season (both individual and team points)\n            # Get individual player data (for scores and averages)\n            individual_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }\n            \n            individual_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=individual_filters)\n            \n            # Get team bonus data (for team points)\n            team_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }\n            \n            team_bonus_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=team_filters)\n            \n            # Use individual data for the main league data (scores and averages)\n            league_data = individual_data",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 42,
            "line": 1234
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 108,
            "line": 1193
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "app/services/league_service.py"
      },
      "score": 0.1582030951976776
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "function updateTableTeamDetails() {\r\n    const selectedSeason = document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedLeague = document.querySelector('input[name=\"league\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedWeek = document.querySelector('input[name=\"week\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedTeam = document.querySelector('input[name=\"team\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedView = document.querySelector('input[name=\"teamView\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    let endpoint = '/league/get_team_week_details_table';\r\n    let viewMode = undefined;\r\n    if (selectedView === 'new') {\r\n        endpoint = '/league/get_team_individual_scores_table';\r\n    } else if (selectedView === 'newFull') {\r\n        endpoint = '/league/get_team_week_head_to_head_table';\r\n        viewMode = 'full';\r\n    }\r\n    if (!selectedSeason || !selectedLeague || !selectedWeek || !selectedTeam) {\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    // Update header\r\n    const matchDayText = window.translations ? (window.translations['match_day_label'] || 'Match Day') : 'Match Day';\r\n    document.getElementById('teamDetailsMetadata').innerHTML = &#96;${selectedTeam} - ${matchDayText} ${selectedWeek}&#96;;\r\n    // Use fetchAndRenderTable for consistent table rendering\r\n    const params = {\r\n        season: selectedSeason,\r\n        league: selectedLeague,\r\n        week: selectedWeek,\r\n        team: selectedTeam,\r\n        options: {\r\n            disablePositionCircle: true,\r\n            mergeCells: false,\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true\r\n        }\r\n    };\r\n    if (viewMode) {\r\n        params.view_mode = viewMode;\r\n    }\r\n    fetchAndRenderTable('teamTableWeekDetails', endpoint, params);\r\n}\r\n\r\n",

        "originalContents": "function updateTableTeamDetails() {\r\n    const selectedSeason = document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedLeague = document.querySelector('input[name=\"league\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedWeek = document.querySelector('input[name=\"week\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedTeam = document.querySelector('input[name=\"team\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedView = document.querySelector('input[name=\"teamView\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    let endpoint = '/league/get_team_week_details_table';\r\n    let viewMode = undefined;\r\n    if (selectedView === 'new') {\r\n        endpoint = '/league/get_team_individual_scores_table';\r\n    } else if (selectedView === 'newFull') {\r\n        endpoint = '/league/get_team_week_head_to_head_table';\r\n        viewMode = 'full';\r\n    }\r\n    if (!selectedSeason || !selectedLeague || !selectedWeek || !selectedTeam) {\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    // Update header\r\n    const matchDayText = window.translations ? (window.translations['match_day_label'] || 'Match Day') : 'Match Day';\r\n    document.getElementById('teamDetailsMetadata').innerHTML = &#96;${selectedTeam} - ${matchDayText} ${selectedWeek}&#96;;\r\n    // Use fetchAndRenderTable for consistent table rendering\r\n    const params = {\r\n        season: selectedSeason,\r\n        league: selectedLeague,\r\n        week: selectedWeek,\r\n        team: selectedTeam,\r\n        options: {\r\n            disablePositionCircle: true,\r\n            mergeCells: false,\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true\r\n        }\r\n    };\r\n    if (viewMode) {\r\n        params.view_mode = viewMode;\r\n    }\r\n    fetchAndRenderTable('teamTableWeekDetails', endpoint, params);\r\n}\r\n\r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\r\n                    // Select the first available season and update weeks immediately\r\n                    const firstSeason = seasons.length \u003e 0 ? seasons[0] : null;\r\n                    if (firstSeason) {\r\n                        const selectedTeam = document.getElementById('teamSelect').value;\r\n                        // If 'All' is present, prefer it, else use firstSeason\r\n                        const selectedSeason = document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value || firstSeason;\r\n                        updateWeekButtons(selectedTeam, selectedSeason);\r\n                    }\r\n                });\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        // In updateTeamSelect, after calling updateSeasonButtons(selectedTeam), also update weeks\r\n        function updateTeamSelect(teams) {\r\n            const select = document.getElementById('teamSelect');\r\n            select.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n                \u003coption value=\"\"\u003eBitte wählen...\u003c/option\u003e\r\n                ${teams.map(team =\u003e &#96;\r\n                    \u003coption value=\"${team}\"\u003e${team}\u003c/option\u003e\r\n                &#96;).join('')}\r\n            &#96;;\r\n\r\n            // Remove old event listener first to prevent duplicates\r\n            const newSelect = select.cloneNode(true);\r\n            select.parentNode.replaceChild(newSelect, select);\r\n\r\n            // Add event listener to new select\r\n            newSelect.addEventListener('change', function() {\r\n                const selectedTeam = this.value;\r\n                console.log('Selected team:', selectedTeam); // Debug log\r\n                if (selectedTeam) {\r\n                    updateSeasonButtons(selectedTeam);\r\n                    updateMessageVisibility();\r\n                    updateTeamHistory(selectedTeam);\r\n            \r\n                    updateLeagueComparison(selectedTeam); // Call new function\r\n                    loadSpecialMatches(selectedTeam);\r\n                }\r\n            });\r\n        }\r\n",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "\r\n                    // Select the first available season and update weeks immediately\r\n                    const firstSeason = seasons.length \u003e 0 ? seasons[0] : null;\r\n                    if (firstSeason) {\r\n                        const selectedTeam = document.getElementById('teamSelect').value;\r\n                        // If 'All' is present, prefer it, else use firstSeason\r\n                        const selectedSeason = document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value || firstSeason;\r\n                        updateWeekButtons(selectedTeam, selectedSeason);\r\n                    }\r\n                });\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        // In updateTeamSelect, after calling updateSeasonButtons(selectedTeam), also update weeks\r\n        function updateTeamSelect(teams) {\r\n            const select = document.getElementById('teamSelect');\r\n            select.innerHTML = &#96;\r\n                \u003coption value=\"\"\u003eBitte wählen...\u003c/option\u003e\r\n                ${teams.map(team =\u003e &#96;\r\n                    \u003coption value=\"${team}\"\u003e${team}\u003c/option\u003e\r\n                &#96;).join('')}\r\n            &#96;;\r\n\r\n            // Remove old event listener first to prevent duplicates\r\n            const newSelect = select.cloneNode(true);\r\n            select.parentNode.replaceChild(newSelect, select);\r\n\r\n            // Add event listener to new select\r\n            newSelect.addEventListener('change', function() {\r\n                const selectedTeam = this.value;\r\n                console.log('Selected team:', selectedTeam); // Debug log\r\n                if (selectedTeam) {\r\n                    updateSeasonButtons(selectedTeam);\r\n                    updateMessageVisibility();\r\n                    updateTeamHistory(selectedTeam);\r\n            \r\n                    updateLeagueComparison(selectedTeam); // Call new function\r\n                    loadSpecialMatches(selectedTeam);\r\n                }\r\n            });\r\n        }\r\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "import pandas as pd\r\n\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns, ColumnsExtra\r\nfrom abc import ABC, abstractmethod\r\nfrom typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\nclass DataAdapter(ABC):\r\n    \"\"\"Abstract base class for data adapters\"\"\"\r\n    \r\n    # Define standard operators that all adapters should support\r\n    OPERATORS = {\r\n        \"eq\": \"Equal to\",\r\n        \"ne\": \"Not equal to\",\r\n        \"lt\": \"Less than\",\r\n        \"le\": \"Less than or equal to\",\r\n        \"gt\": \"Greater than\",\r\n        \"ge\": \"Greater than or equal to\",\r\n        \"in\": \"In list\",\r\n        \"not_in\": \"Not in list\",\r\n        \"contains\": \"String contains\",\r\n        \"startswith\": \"String starts with\",\r\n        \"endswith\": \"String ends with\"\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    @abstractmethod\r\n    def get_player_data(self, player_name: str, season: str) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        \"\"\"Get player data for a specific season\"\"\"\r\n        pass\r\n\r\n    @abstractmethod\r\n    def get_all_players(self) -\u003e List[str]:\r\n        pass\r\n\r\n    @abstractmethod\r\n    def get_filtered_data(self, filters: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        \"\"\"Get filtered data based on provided filters\r\n        \r\n        Args:\r\n            filters: Dictionary of column names to filter dictionaries\r\n                   Each filter dictionary should have:\r\n                   - 'value': The value to filter by\r\n                   - 'operator': The operator to use (must be one of OPERATORS keys)\r\n        \r\n        Returns:\r\n            DataFrame containing the filtered data\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        pass\r\n\r\n    @abstractmethod\r\n    def get_league_standings(self, league_name: str, season: str) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        \"\"\"Get league standings for a specific season\"\"\"\r\n        pass",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "import pandas as pd\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "from data_access.schema import Columns, ColumnsExtra\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "from abc import ABC, abstractmethod\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "from typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "class DataAdapter(ABC):\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10,
            "text": "    \"\"\"Abstract base class for data adapters\"\"\"\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "    \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12,
            "text": "    # Define standard operators that all adapters should support\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "    OPERATORS = {\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14,
            "text": "        \"eq\": \"Equal to\",\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "        \"ne\": \"Not equal to\",\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 16,
            "text": "        \"lt\": \"Less than\",\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 17,
            "text": "        \"le\": \"Less than or equal to\",\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 18,
            "text": "        \"gt\": \"Greater than\",\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 19,
            "text": "        \"ge\": \"Greater than or equal to\",\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 20,
            "text": "        \"in\": \"In list\",\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 21,
            "text": "        \"not_in\": \"Not in list\",\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 22,
            "text": "        \"contains\": \"String contains\",\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "        \"startswith\": \"String starts with\",\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 24,
            "text": "        \"endswith\": \"String ends with\"\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 25,
            "text": "    }\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 26,
            "text": "    \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 27,
            "text": "    @abstractmethod\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 28,
            "text": "    def get_player_data(self, player_name: str, season: str) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "        \"\"\"Get player data for a specific season\"\"\"\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "        pass\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32,
            "text": "    @abstractmethod\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "    def get_all_players(self) -\u003e List[str]:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34,
            "text": "        pass\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "    @abstractmethod\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "    def get_filtered_data(self, filters: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38,
            "text": "        \"\"\"Get filtered data based on provided filters\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "        \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "        Args:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "            filters: Dictionary of column names to filter dictionaries\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "                   Each filter dictionary should have:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "                   - 'value': The value to filter by\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "                   - 'operator': The operator to use (must be one of OPERATORS keys)\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "        \r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46,
            "text": "        Returns:\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47,
            "text": "            DataFrame containing the filtered data\r"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "        \"\"\"\r"

        "originalContents": "import pandas as pd\r\n\r\nfrom data_access.schema import Columns, ColumnsExtra\r\nfrom abc import ABC, abstractmethod\r\nfrom typing import List, Dict, Any, Optional\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\nclass DataAdapter(ABC):\r\n    \"\"\"Abstract base class for data adapters\"\"\"\r\n    \r\n    # Define standard operators that all adapters should support\r\n    OPERATORS = {\r\n        \"eq\": \"Equal to\",\r\n        \"ne\": \"Not equal to\",\r\n        \"lt\": \"Less than\",\r\n        \"le\": \"Less than or equal to\",\r\n        \"gt\": \"Greater than\",\r\n        \"ge\": \"Greater than or equal to\",\r\n        \"in\": \"In list\",\r\n        \"not_in\": \"Not in list\",\r\n        \"contains\": \"String contains\",\r\n        \"startswith\": \"String starts with\",\r\n        \"endswith\": \"String ends with\"\r\n    }\r\n    \r\n    @abstractmethod\r\n    def get_player_data(self, player_name: str, season: str) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        \"\"\"Get player data for a specific season\"\"\"\r\n        pass\r\n\r\n    @abstractmethod\r\n    def get_all_players(self) -\u003e List[str]:\r\n        pass\r\n\r\n    @abstractmethod\r\n    def get_filtered_data(self, filters: Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        \"\"\"Get filtered data based on provided filters\r\n        \r\n        Args:\r\n            filters: Dictionary of column names to filter dictionaries\r\n                   Each filter dictionary should have:\r\n                   - 'value': The value to filter by\r\n                   - 'operator': The operator to use (must be one of OPERATORS keys)\r\n        \r\n        Returns:\r\n            DataFrame containing the filtered data\r\n        \"\"\"\r\n        pass\r\n\r\n    @abstractmethod\r\n    def get_league_standings(self, league_name: str, season: str) -\u003e pd.DataFrame:\r\n        \"\"\"Get league standings for a specific season\"\"\"\r\n        pass",
```

```diff
@@ -458,2 +458,72 @@
          )
      
+     def get_matches(self, team: str = None, season: str = None, league: str = None, opponent_team_name: str = None) -&gt; pd.DataFrame:
+         """Get matches data with team and opponent scores
+         
+         Args:
+             team: Team name to filter by
+             season: Season to filter by
+             league: League to filter by
+             opponent_team_name: Opponent team name to filter by
+             
+         Returns:
+             DataFrame with columns: league, season, week, team_name, round_number, 
+             score (team score), opponent_team_name, opponent_score (opponent team score)
+         """
+         if self.df is None or self.df.empty:
+             return pd.DataFrame()
+         
+         # Build filters for team data
+         team_filters = {
+             Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}
+         }
+         
+         if team:
+             team_filters[Columns.team_name] = {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'}
+         if season:
+             team_filters[Columns.season] = {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'}
+         if league:
+             team_filters[Columns.league_name] = {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'}
+         if opponent_team_name:
+             team_filters[Columns.team_name_opponent] = {'value': opponent_team_name, 'operator': 'eq'}
+         
+         # Get team data
+         team_data = self.get_filtered_data(filters=team_filters)
+         
+         if team_data.empty:
+             return pd.DataFrame()
+         
+         # Create result DataFrame with required columns
+         result_columns = [
+             Columns.league_name, Columns.season, Columns.week, 
+             Columns.team_name, Columns.round_number, Columns.score, 
+             Columns.team_name_opponent
+         ]
+         
+         result_df = team_data[result_columns].copy()
+         
+         # Add opponent_score column
+         result_df['opponent_score'] = 0
+         
+         # For each match (combination of season, league, week, round_number), 
+         # find the opponent's score and add it to the result
+         for idx, row in result_df.iterrows():
+             # Find the opponent's data for this specific match
+             opponent_filters = {
+                 Columns.season: {'value': row[Columns.season], 'operator': 'eq'},
+                 Columns.league_name: {'value': row[Columns.league_name], 'operator': 'eq'},
+                 Columns.week: {'value': row[Columns.week], 'operator': 'eq'},
+                 Columns.round_number: {'value': row[Columns.round_number], 'operator': 'eq'},
+                 Columns.team_name: {'value': row[Columns.team_name_opponent], 'operator': 'eq'},
+                 Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}
+             }
+             
+             opponent_data = self.get_filtered_data(filters=opponent_filters)
+             
+             if not opponent_data.empty:
+                 # Get the opponent's score for this match
+                 opponent_score = opponent_data[Columns.score].iloc[0]
+                 result_df.at[idx, 'opponent_score'] = opponent_score
+         
+         return result_df
+     
```

</details>

```diff
+ #!/usr/bin/env python3
+ """
+ Test script for the new get_matches functionality
+ """
+ 
+ import sys
+ import os
+ sys.path.append(os.path.dirname(__file__))
+ 
+ from business_logic.server import Server
+ from data_access.schema import Columns
+ 
+ def test_get_matches():
+     """Test the get_matches functionality"""
+     print("Testing get_matches functionality...")
+     
+     # Initialize server
+     server = Server()
+     
+     # Test 1: Get all matches for a team
+     print("\n1. Testing get_matches for a team...")
+     team_matches = server.get_matches(team="Team A")
+     print(f"Found {len(team_matches)} matches for Team A")
+     if not team_matches.empty:
+         print("Sample match data:")
+         print(team_matches.head())
+         print(f"Columns: {list(team_matches.columns)}")
+     
+     # Test 2: Get matches for a specific season and league
+     print("\n2. Testing get_matches with season and league filters...")
+     filtered_matches = server.get_matches(team="Team A", season="23/24", league="Major League")
+     print(f"Found {len(filtered_matches)} matches for Team A in 23/24 Major League")
+     if not filtered_matches.empty:
+         print("Sample filtered match data:")
+         print(filtered_matches.head())
+     
+     # Test 3: Test special matches functionality
+     print("\n3. Testing get_special_matches...")
+     special_matches = server.get_special_matches(team_name="Team A", amount=3)
+     print("Special matches result:")
+     for category, matches in special_matches.items():
+         print(f"{category}: {len(matches)} matches")
+         if matches:
+             print(f"  Sample: {matches[0]}")
+     
+     print("\nTest completed!")
+ 
+ if __name__ == "__main__":
+     test_get_matches() 
```

_**Agent**_

## Summary

I have successfully implemented the `get_matches` method and the necessary call stack to delegate data wrangling from the service layer to the data adapter. Here's what was implemented:

### 1. **Data Adapter Interface** (`data_access/adapters/data_adapter.py`)
- Added the abstract `get_matches` method to the `DataAdapter` base class
- Method signature: `get_matches(team: str = None, season: str = None, league: str = None, opponent_team_name: str = None) -> pd.DataFrame`
- Returns DataFrame with columns: `league`, `season`, `week`, `team_name`, `round_number`, `score` (team score), `opponent_team_name`, `opponent_score` (opponent team score)

### 2. **Pandas Data Adapter Implementation** (`data_access/adapters/data_adapter_pandas.py`)
- Implemented the `get_matches` method in `DataAdapterPandas`
- The method:
  - Filters data based on provided parameters (team, season, league, opponent_team_name)
  - Only includes computed data (`computed_data = True`)
  - For each match, finds the opponent's score by querying the database
  - Returns a DataFrame with both team and opponent scores for easy analysis

### 3. **Server Layer** (`business_logic/server.py`)
- Added the `get_matches` method to the `Server` class
- Acts as a simple pass-through to the data adapter
- Maintains the same interface as the data adapter

### 4. **Service Layer Updates** (`app/services/team_service.py`)
- **`get_special_matches`**: Completely refactored to use the new `get_matches` method
  - Removed complex data wrangling logic
  - Now simply calls `self.server.get_matches()` and processes the results
  - Much cleaner and more maintainable code

- **`get_clutch_performance`**: Updated to use `get_matches`
  - Removed manual opponent data lookup
  - Now uses the pre-computed opponent scores from the data adapter

- **`get_streak_analysis`**: Updated to use `get_matches`
  - Simplified logic by using the pre-computed opponent scores
  - Removed complex data filtering and joining

### 5. **Benefits of the New Implementation**

1. **Reduced Complexity**: The service layer no longer needs to handle complex data wrangling
2. **Better Separation of Concerns**: Data access logic is now properly encapsulated in the data adapter
3. **Improved Performance**: The data adapter can optimize the queries and avoid redundant database calls
4. **Easier Testing**: The data adapter methods can be tested independently
5. **Reusability**: The `get_matches` method can be used by other services that need match data
6. **Consistency**: All match-related data access goes through the same method

### 6. **Data Structure**
The new `get_matches` method returns a DataFrame with the following columns:
- `League`: League name
- `Season`: Season identifier  
- `Week`: Week number
- `Team`: Team name
- `Round Number`: Round number
- `Score`: Team's accumulated score
- `Opponent`: Opponent team name
- `opponent_score`: Opponent's accumulated score

This structure makes it easy to calculate margins, find min/max scores, and perform other match-related analyses without complex data manipulation in the service layer.

The implementation successfully delegates the data wrangling complexity from the service layer to the data adapter, making the code more maintainable and following better architectural patterns.

---

```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,95 @@
- 
+ #!/usr/bin/env python3
+ """
+ Debug script to test the get_matches functionality
+ """
+ 
+ import sys
+ import os
+ sys.path.append(os.path.dirname(__file__))
+ 
+ from business_logic.server import Server
+ from data_access.schema import Columns
+ 
+ def debug_get_matches():
+     """Debug the get_matches functionality"""
+     print("Debugging get_matches functionality...")
+     
+     # Initialize server
+     server = Server()
+     
+     # Test 1: Check if we can get any data at all
+     print("\n1. Testing basic data access...")
+     try:
+         # Get some sample data to see what's available
+         sample_data = server.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(filters={
+             Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}
+         })
+         print(f"Total computed data rows: {len(sample_data)}")
+         if not sample_data.empty:
+             print("Sample columns:", list(sample_data.columns))
+             print("Sample teams:", sample_data[Columns.team_name].unique()[:5].tolist())
+             print("Sample seasons:", sample_data[Columns.season].unique()[:5].tolist())
+             print("Sample leagues:", sample_data[Columns.league_name].unique()[:5].tolist())
+     except Exception as e:
+         print(f"Error accessing data: {e}")
+         return
+     
+     # Test 2: Test get_matches with a specific team
+     print("\n2. Testing get_matches for a specific team...")
+     try:
+         # Get first available team
+         teams = sample_data[Columns.team_name].unique()
+         if len(teams) &gt; 0:
+             test_team = teams[0]
+             print(f"Testing with team: {test_team}")
+             
+             team_matches = server.get_matches(team=test_team)
+             print(f"Found {len(team_matches)} matches for {test_team}")
+             
+             if not team_matches.empty:
+                 print("Match data columns:", list(team_matches.columns))
+                 print("Sample match data:")
+                 print(team_matches.head())
+                 
+                 # Check if opponent_score is populated
+                 non_zero_opponent_scores = team_matches[team_matches['opponent_score'] &gt; 0]
+                 print(f"Matches with opponent scores: {len(non_zero_opponent_scores)}")
+                 
+                 if len(non_zero_opponent_scores) &gt; 0:
+                     print("Sample with opponent scores:")
+                     print(non_zero_opponent_scores.head())
+             else:
+                 print("No matches found!")
+         else:
+             print("No teams found in data!")
+     except Exception as e:
+         print(f"Error in get_matches: {e}")
+     
+     # Test 3: Test clutch performance
+     print("\n3. Testing clutch performance...")
+     try:
+         if len(teams) &gt; 0:
+             test_team = teams[0]
+             # Get first available season and league for this team
+             team_data = sample_data[sample_data[Columns.team_name] == test_team]
+             if not team_data.empty:
+                 test_season = team_data[Columns.season].iloc[0]
+                 test_league = team_data[Columns.league_name].iloc[0]
+                 
+                 print(f"Testing clutch performance for {test_team} in {test_season} {test_league}")
+                 
+                 from app.services.team_service import TeamService
+                 team_service = TeamService()
+                 clutch_data = team_service.get_clutch_performance(test_team, test_league, test_season)
+                 
+                 print("Clutch performance result:")
+                 print(clutch_data)
+             else:
+                 print("No data found for test team!")
+     except Exception as e:
+         print(f"Error in clutch performance: {e}")
+         import traceback
+         traceback.print_exc()
+ 
+ if __name__ == "__main__":
+     debug_get_matches() 
```

The issue with clutch performance and consistency not showing up is likely that the JavaScript functions are not being called or there's an issue with the data being returned from those endpoints.

From the debug output, I can see that the team history and league comparison are working, but I don't see any calls to:
- `/team/get_clutch_analysis`
- `/team/get_consistency_metrics` 
- `/team/get_streak_analysis`

This suggests that the JavaScript functions `updateClutchAnalysis`, `updateConsistencyMetrics`, and `updateStreakAnalysis` are not being called.

Looking at the template code, these functions should be called when a season is selected. The issue might be in the logic that determines when to call these functions. Let me check the JavaScript logic in the template:
