```diff
@@ -1,1 +1,133 @@
- 
+ #!/usr/bin/env python3
+ """
+ Script to extract data from BYL_Maenner-5-6.xlsx and map it to the existing CSV format.
+ """
+ 
+ import pandas as pd
+ import numpy as np
+ from datetime import datetime
+ import re
+ 
+ def extract_excel_data():
+     """Extract data from Excel file and map to CSV format."""
+     
+     # Read the Excel file
+     print("Reading Excel file...")
+     try:
+         # Try to read all sheets to understand the structure
+         excel_file = pd.ExcelFile('BYL_Maenner-5-6.xlsx')
+         print(f"Found sheets: {excel_file.sheet_names}")
+         
+         # Read the first sheet to understand structure
+         df = pd.read_excel('BYL_Maenner-5-6.xlsx', sheet_name=0)
+         print(f"Excel file shape: {df.shape}")
+         print(f"Excel columns: {list(df.columns)}")
+         print("\nFirst few rows:")
+         print(df.head())
+         
+         # Let's examine the data structure more carefully
+         print("\nData types:")
+         print(df.dtypes)
+         
+         print("\nSample data from first few rows:")
+         for i in range(min(5, len(df))):
+             print(f"Row {i}: {df.iloc[i].to_dict()}")
+             
+     except Exception as e:
+         print(f"Error reading Excel file: {e}")
+         return None
+     
+     # Now let's try to extract and map the data
+     extracted_data = []
+     
+     # We'll need to infer the structure based on what we see
+     # Let me create a mapping function
+     def map_excel_to_csv(excel_df):
+         """Map Excel data to CSV format."""
+         
+         # Target CSV columns:
+         # Season;Week;Date;League;Players per Team;Location;Round Number;Match Number;Team;Position;Player;Player ID;Opponent;Score;Points;Input Data;Computed Data
+         
+         csv_data = []
+         
+         # Extract basic information from filename or data
+         season = "24/25"  # Based on filename suggesting 2024-25 season
+         week = "5-6"      # From filename
+         league = "BYL"    # From filename
+         players_per_team = 4  # Standard for bowling
+         location = "Unknown"  # Will try to extract from data
+         round_number = 0   # Default
+         match_number = 0   # Default
+         
+         # Try to extract team and player information
+         for idx, row in excel_df.iterrows():
+             # Skip empty rows
+             if row.isna().all():
+                 continue
+                 
+             # Convert row to string for easier processing
+             row_str = str(row.to_dict())
+             
+             # Try to extract team names (look for patterns like "Team Name" or "City Name")
+             team_match = re.search(r'([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+\d+)?)', row_str)
+             team_name = team_match.group(1) if team_match else "Unknown Team"
+             
+             # Try to extract player names (look for patterns like "First Last" or "Name")
+             player_match = re.search(r'([A-Z][a-z]+\s+[A-Z][a-z]+)', row_str)
+             player_name = player_match.group(1) if player_match else "Unknown Player"
+             
+             # Try to extract scores (look for numbers that could be bowling scores)
+             score_match = re.search(r'(\d{3,4})', row_str)
+             score = int(score_match.group(1)) if score_match else 0
+             
+             # Generate a simple player ID
+             player_id = hash(player_name) % 100000
+             
+             # Determine opponent (this is complex, we'll need to understand the structure better)
+             opponent = "Unknown Opponent"
+             
+             # Create CSV row
+             csv_row = {
+                 'Season': season,
+                 'Week': week,
+                 'Date': '2024-12-01',  # Default date, will need to be extracted
+                 'League': league,
+                 'Players per Team': players_per_team,
+                 'Location': location,
+                 'Round Number': round_number,
+                 'Match Number': match_number,
+                 'Team': team_name,
+                 'Position': idx % 4,  # Assume 4 players per team
+                 'Player': player_name,
+                 'Player ID': player_id,
+                 'Opponent': opponent,
+                 'Score': score,
+                 'Points': '',  # Empty for now
+                 'Input Data': 'True',
+                 'Computed Data': 'False'
+             }
+             
+             csv_data.append(csv_row)
+         
+         return csv_data
+     
+     # Try to map the data
+     print("\nAttempting to map data...")
+     mapped_data = map_excel_to_csv(df)
+     
+     if mapped_data:
+         # Create DataFrame and save to CSV
+         result_df = pd.DataFrame(mapped_data)
+         result_df.to_csv('extracted_league_data.csv', index=False, sep=';')
+         print(f"\nExtracted {len(mapped_data)} rows to extracted_league_data.csv")
+         print("\nSample extracted data:")
+         print(result_df.head())
+     else:
+         print("No data could be extracted.")
+     
+     return mapped_data
+ 
+ if __name__ == "__main__":
+     print("Starting Excel data extraction...")
+     extracted_data = extract_excel_data()
+     print("Extraction complete!") 
```

_**User (2025-07-27 13:43:35Z)**_

okay, so the format is basically:
the score sheet of multiple teams a below each other. see the repetitve pattern all 30 rows?
the first cols are all hidden, we start looking at data at col "z".
the following is true for each team's segement:
- in the first row we can see the name of hte league "Bayernliga Herren" --> League == BayL
- in 2nd row we see Ort: Dettelbach Extreme Bowling --> Location; and Spieltag: 6 --> Week
the we have the team specific data:
- 3rd row: Team Name "BC Comet Nürnberg"
- row 5 tells us which Round Number the score will asscosiated with
- row 6 tells us what the following cols will mean, you can see where player name == Player, Rl.-Nr. == PLayer ID, Pins == Score and Pkt. == Points are located.
- row 7-9 who played a position 1 --> match number == 1 in the given game? there might be more than 1 player name. we learn from the matrix player name / Spiel at which round the player player wich score. per position ther will be only 1 player. 
- rows 10-12, 13-15, 16-18 are like above but for positions 2-4 in the team
- row 19 Gesamt == team total score
- row 23 the name of the opposing team each round
- rows 25-29 the respective results of the opposing team at pos 1-4 and team total. this can be used for double checking the other teams score sheet, since the results should be mirrored on their sheet.

can you first understand, verify, eventually correct and then apply this logic to the parser?

---

_**Agent (2025-07-27 13:43:35Z)**_

Perfect! Now I understand the structure. Let me analyze and verify your description, then create a proper parser:

## **Structure Analysis:**

### **Team Segment Pattern (30 rows per team):**
1. **Row 1**: League name "Bayernliga Herren" → League = "BayL"
2. **Row 2**: Location "Dettelbach Extreme Bowling" + Week "Spieltag: 6" → Week = "6"
3. **Row 3**: Team name "BC Comet Nürnberg"
4. **Row 5**: Round Number
5. **Row 6**: Column headers (Player, Player ID, Score, Points)
6. **Rows 7-9**: Position 1 players (multiple players, one per round)
7. **Rows 10-12**: Position 2 players
8. **Rows 13-15**: Position 3 players  
9. **Rows 16-18**: Position 4 players
10. **Row 19**: Team total score
11. **Row 23**: Opposing team name
12. **Rows 25-29**: Opposing team results (for verification)

### **Key Insights:**
- **Column Z**: Start of data (hidden columns before)
- **Multiple rounds per match**: Each position has multiple players (one per round)
- **Match Number**: Derived from round number
- **Opponent data**: Available for cross-verification

Let me create a proper parser based on this structure:

_**Agent**_

Perfect! I've fixed the row indexing issue. Here are the key corrections:

## **Row Indexing Fixes:**

### **1. Proper 0-based vs 1-based Mapping:**
- ✅ **Row 1** (1-based) = `iloc[0]` (0-based)
- ✅ **Row 2** (1-based) = `iloc[1]` (0-based)  
- ✅ **Row 3** (1-based) = `iloc[2]` (0-based)
- ✅ **Row 5** (1-based) = `iloc[4]` (0-based)
- ✅ **Row 6** (1-based) = `iloc[5]` (0-based)
- ✅ **Row 23** (1-based) = `iloc[22]` (0-based)

### **2. Position Rows Corrected:**
- ✅ **Position 1**: rows 7-9 (1-based) = `iloc[6:9]` (0-based)
- ✅ **Position 2**: rows 10-12 (1-based) = `iloc[9:12]` (0-based)
- ✅ **Position 3**: rows 13-15 (1-based) = `iloc[12:15]` (0-based)
- ✅ **Position 4**: rows 16-18 (1-based) = `iloc[15:18]` (0-based)

### **3. Clear Documentation:**
- ✅ **Added comments**: Show both 1-based and 0-based row numbers
- ✅ **Better logging**: Shows actual row numbers being processed
- ✅ **Consistent indexing**: All row references now properly converted

### **4. Key Changes:**
```python
# Before (incorrect):
pos_rows = team_data.iloc[start_row_idx-1:end_row_idx]

# After (correct):
pos_rows = team_data.iloc[start_row_idx:end_row_idx+1]
```

Now the parser should correctly:
1. **Read the right rows** for each team segment
2. **Extract data from the correct positions** (rows 7-18 for player data)
3. **Find the opponent** in row 23
4. **Map all data properly** to the CSV format

The row offset issue is now fixed! Try running the script again - it should properly extract the data from the correct rows.

- ✅ **Check Pins value**: Only add to CSV if player has valid score
- ✅ **Skip non-participants**: If Pins is NaN, skip that round

### **3. Correct Round Number Logic:**

- ✅ **Round Number**: Round 1-3 within each position

- ✅ **Clear round tracking**: Shows which round within position

```python

# Row 7: "Spieler 1" - Position 1, Round 2  
# Row 8: "Spieler 1" - Position 1, Round 3
# Row 9: "Spieler 2" - Position 2, Round 1
# etc.
```

_**User (2025-07-27 13:43:36Z)**_

1. yes this is lane information, we dont use it in the db -> drop 
2. Spiel 1...n is the Round Number. each week has as many rounds as there are opponents in the league. each team plays against each other team each week. the round number defines at which round 2 teams competetd against each other.
3. this means each Spiel "n" has information on the score (Pins) and the earned Points (Pkt.). the results of all payers that participated in the given round are documented in these cols. 
4. yes. potentially there are up to 3 players per position, depending on how the team chose to sub during the day. 
5. correct
6. yes, if there is a proper number it means the player participated
7. that is the team total scpre and should ne the sum of all individual resuls in this col
8. there are 9 teams, each team should fill 30 rows. you can see the begining of a team section when you encounter "Team-Nr."

Perfect! Now I've fixed the column detection to properly handle individual rounds vs. totals:

## **Key Fixes:**

### **1. Proper Column Detection:**
- ✅ **Excludes "Gesamt" columns**: `"gesamt" not in value_str`
- ✅ **Only individual rounds**: Finds "Pins" and "Pkt." columns for each Spiel
- ✅ **Verifies 9 rounds**: Checks that we have exactly 9 score columns

### **2. Two-Pass Column Detection:**
```python
# First pass: Find Name and ID columns
for col_idx, value in header_row.items():
    if "name" in value_str:
        name_col = col_idx
    elif "rl" in value_str:
        id_col = col_idx

# Second pass: Find individual round columns (exclude totals)
for col_idx, value in header_row.items():
    if "pins" in value_str and "gesamt" not in value_str:
        score_cols.append(col_idx)
    elif "pkt" in value_str and "gesamt" not in value_str:
        points_cols.append(col_idx)
```

### **3. Validation:**
- ✅ **Warns if not 9 rounds**: `if len(score_cols) != 9:`
- ✅ **Shows rounds per position**: Verifies each position has 9 rounds
- ✅ **Better logging**: Shows exactly which columns are found

### **4. Expected Result:**
- ✅ **9 individual rounds** per position (Spiel 1-9)
- ✅ **No total scores** included as individual rounds
- ✅ **Proper round numbering** (1-9, not including totals)

Now the parser should correctly:
1. **Find 9 individual round columns** (excluding "Gesamt")
2. **Extract individual scores** for each Spiel 1-9
3. **Ignore total columns** that would skew the data
4. **Verify 9 rounds per position** in the output

This should give you exactly 9 games per position as expected!

_**User (2025-07-27 13:43:35Z)**_

we are getting closer, but the collected points dont quite add up. it seems you still got issues with counting points of players that are not in the first row of a positions triplet of rows. you hae nailed collecting the data for "Schanzer Ingolstadt 1" which happen to have played with the same roster throughout the day. for team "BC Comet Nürnberg" there are 10.5 missing which is exaclty the amount of points earned by players subbing at the positions (which means, the row where the score is stored differs from the points earned, since the points earned are stored in a merged cell (3 rows by 1 col)

---

_**Agent (2025-07-27 13:43:35Z)**_

Ah, I see the issue! You're absolutely right. The problem is with **merged cells** for points. When players sub in/out during the day, the points are stored in a **merged cell that spans 3 rows** (the entire position), but I'm only looking at the first row of each position.

So if:
- **Row 7**: Player A scores 190, points stored in merged cell (rows 7-9)
- **Row 8**: Player B scores 201, points stored in merged cell (rows 7-9) 
- **Row 9**: Player C scores 147, points stored in merged cell (rows 7-9)

I need to check **all 3 rows** of each position to find where the points are actually stored, not just the first row.

Let me fix this:

_**Agent**_

Perfect! Now I've fixed the points collection logic:

## **Key Fix:**

### **Before (Wrong):**
- ❌ `points = str(row[points_col])` - Only checked the current player's row
- ❌ **Missed merged cells** - Points could be in any of the 3 rows of the position

### **After (Correct):**
- ✅ **Check all 3 rows** of the position: `range(start_row, end_row + 1)`
- ✅ **Find first non-empty points cell**: `if pd.notna(check_row[points_col])`
- ✅ **Handle merged cells properly** - Points stored in merged cell spanning all 3 rows

## **How It Works:**

For each position (3 rows), when a player participates:
1. **Get score** from the player's specific row
2. **Find points** by checking all 3 rows of that position for the merged cell
3. **Use first found points** (since merged cells typically appear in the first row)

## **Expected Results:**

Now for BC Comet Nürnberg, you should see:
- ✅ **All individual player points** correctly collected from merged cells
- ✅ **Team total points** that match the sum of individual points
- ✅ **No missing 10.5 points** - all substitution points will be captured

The debug output should show:
```
Round 1: Score=190, Points=0.5, Opponent=BC EMAX Unterföhring 2
Round 2: Score=201, Points=0, Opponent=SG Rottendorf 1
Round 3: Score=147, Points=0, Opponent=BK München 3
...
```

And the team totals should now correctly sum up all the individual points, including those from substituted players!

Now I'll add the JavaScript for the data source selector to the base template:


_**Agent**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="search_replace">

<details><summary>Tool use: **search_replace** • Edit file: app/templates/base.html</summary>

**Chunk 1**
Lines added: 69, lines removed: 0

```diff
@@ -342,4 +342,73 @@
              return translations[key] || key;
          }
+         
+         // Data source selector functionality
+         document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {
+             setupDataSourceSelector();
+         });
+         
+         // Setup data source selector
+         function setupDataSourceSelector() {
+             const dataSourceItems = document.querySelectorAll('.data-source-option');
+             
+             dataSourceItems.forEach(item =&gt; {
+                 item.addEventListener('click', async function(e) {
+                     e.preventDefault();
+                     const source = this.getAttribute('data-source');
+                     await switchDataSource(source);
+                 });
+             });
+             
+             // Load current data source on page load
+             loadCurrentDataSource();
+         }
+         
+         // Switch data source
+         async function switchDataSource(source) {
+             try {
+                 const response = await fetch(&#96;/reload-data?source=${source}&#96;);
+                 
+                 if (response.ok) {
+                     // Update the display
+                     updateDataSourceDisplay(source);
+                     
+                     // Refresh the page to reload data
+                     window.location.reload();
+                 } else {
+                     console.error('Error switching data source');
+                 }
+             } catch (error) {
+                 console.error('Error switching data source:', error);
+             }
+         }
+         
+         // Load current data source
+         async function loadCurrentDataSource() {
+             try {
+                 const response = await fetch('/get-data-source');
+                 const data = await response.json();
+                 
+                 if (data.current_source) {
+                     updateDataSourceDisplay(data.current_source);
+                 }
+             } catch (error) {
+                 console.error('Error loading current data source:', error);
+             }
+         }
+         
+         // Update data source display
+         function updateDataSourceDisplay(source) {
+             const textElement = document.getElementById('currentDataSourceText');
+             
+             if (textElement) {
+                 if (source === 'bowling_ergebnisse.csv') {
+                     textElement.textContent = 'Original Data';
+                 } else if (source === 'bowling_ergebnisse_real.csv') {
+                     textElement.textContent = 'Real Data';
+                 } else {
+                     textElement.textContent = 'Data Source';
+                 }
+             }
+         }
      &lt;/script&gt;
      
```

</details>

</tool-use>

## **Summary of Changes Made:**

### **1. Updated Extraction Script**
- ✅ **Changed output file**: `extracted_league_data.csv` → `data/bowling_ergebnisse_real.csv`
- ✅ **Fixed points collection**: Now handles merged cells properly
- ✅ **Added team totals**: Extracts team scores and points from row 19

### **2. Updated Navbar**
- ✅ **Replaced "Reload Data" button** with dropdown selector
- ✅ **Added two options**: "Original Data" and "Real Data"
- ✅ **Icons and styling**: Uses Bootstrap icons and consistent styling

### **3. Updated Backend**
- ✅ **Enhanced DataManager**: Now tracks current data source
- ✅ **Updated reload route**: Accepts `source` parameter
- ✅ **Added get-data-source route**: Returns current data source

### **4. Added Frontend JavaScript**
- ✅ **Data source selector**: Handles dropdown clicks
- ✅ **Switch data source**: Calls backend with new source
- ✅ **Update display**: Shows current data source in navbar
- ✅ **Page refresh**: Reloads page after switching data source

## **How It Works:**

1. **User clicks dropdown** in navbar → "Data Source"
2. **Selects option** → "Original Data" or "Real Data"
3. **JavaScript calls** `/reload-data?source=bowling_ergebnisse_real.csv`
4. **Backend switches** data source and reloads data
5. **Page refreshes** with new data source
6. **Navbar updates** to show current data source

## **Next Steps:**

_**Agent (2025-07-27 13:43:35Z)**_

You're right! The issue is that the DataManager is a singleton, so when we call `reload_data()` with a new source, it doesn't actually reload the data because the singleton instance already exists. Let me fix this:


_**Agent**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file">

<details><summary>Tool use: **edit_file** • Edit file: app/services/data_manager.py</summary>

I will fix the singleton pattern to properly reload data when the source changes

**Chunk 1**
Lines added: 4, lines removed: 1

```diff
@@ -19,5 +19,8 @@
  
      def reload_data(self, source=None):
-         self._load_data(source)
+         # Force reload even if singleton exists
+         if source:
+             self._current_source = source
+         self._df = pd.read_csv(f'database/data/{self._current_source}', sep=';')
  
      @property
```

</details>

</tool-use>

+     def register_server_instance(self, server_instance):
+         """Register a server instance to be refreshed when data source changes"""
+         if server_instance not in self._server_instances:
+             self._server_instances.append(server_instance)
+ 
+     def _refresh_server_instances(self):
+         """Refresh all registered server instances"""
+         for server in self._server_instances:
+             if hasattr(server, 'refresh_data_adapter'):
+                 server.refresh_data_adapter()

## **Summary of Fixes:**

### **1. Fixed DataManager Singleton Issue**
- ✅ **Force reload**: `reload_data()` now directly updates `_df` instead of calling `_load_data()`
- ✅ **Added server registration**: Server instances register with DataManager
- ✅ **Automatic refresh**: All server instances refresh when data source changes

### **2. Updated Data Adapter Factory**
- ✅ **Dynamic data source**: Uses DataManager's current source instead of hardcoded config
- ✅ **Fallback support**: Falls back to config if DataManager not available

### **3. Enhanced Server Class**
- ✅ **Refresh method**: Added `refresh_data_adapter()` method
- ✅ **Auto-registration**: Registers with DataManager on initialization

### **4. Complete Data Flow**
1. **User selects data source** → Frontend calls `/reload-data?source=bowling_ergebnisse_real.csv`
2. **DataManager updates** → Changes `_current_source` and reloads data
3. **Server instances refresh** → All registered servers get new data adapters
4. **Data adapters use new source** → Factory creates adapters with current source
5. **Page refreshes** → Shows data from new source

## **How It Works Now:**

1. **Data source selector** in navbar triggers backend reload
2. **DataManager** switches to new CSV file and refreshes all server instances
3. **Data adapters** automatically use the new data source
4. **All services** (TeamService, LeagueService, etc.) get fresh data
5. **Frontend refreshes** to show new data

The data should now actually change when you switch between "Original Data" and "Real Data"!

_**Agent**_

So we have 4 services that create Server instances:
1. **TeamService** - creates Server()
2. **PlayerService** - creates Server() 
3. **StatisticsService** - creates Server()
4. **LeagueService** - creates its own data adapter (not Server)

Plus the **LeagueService** creates a **StatisticsService** which creates another Server, so that's 5 total!

The problem is that each service creates its own Server instance, and each Server registers itself with the DataManager. This is inefficient and could cause issues.

Let me fix this by making the Server class a singleton and updating the services to use a shared instance:


_**Agent**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="edit_file">

<details><summary>Tool use: **edit_file** • Edit file: business_logic/server.py</summary>

I will make the Server class a singleton to prevent multiple instances

**Chunk 1**
Lines added: 9, lines removed: 0

```diff
@@ -9,5 +9,14 @@
      # converts basic dataframes to aggregated dataframes
      # forwards aggregated dataframes to app
+     _instance = None
+     
+     def __new__(cls):
+         if cls._instance is None:
+             cls._instance = super(Server, cls).__new__(cls)
+             cls._instance._initialized = False
+         return cls._instance
+     
      def __init__(self):
+         if not self._initialized:
              self.data_adapter = DataAdapterFactory.create_adapter(DataAdapterSelector.PANDAS)
              # Register this server instance with DataManager for automatic refresh
```

**Chunk 2**
Lines added: 1, lines removed: 0

```diff
@@ -19,4 +28,5 @@
                  # DataManager not available, continue without registration
                  pass
+             self._initialized = True
  
      def refresh_data_adapter(self):
```

</details>

</tool-use>

Perfect! Now let's test this. The changes should:

1. **Reduce Server instances** from 5 to 3 (TeamService, PlayerService, StatisticsService)
2. **Add LeagueService** to the registration system
3. **Show clear confirmation** when data source switches

Now when you restart the app, you should see:
- **3 Server instances** instead of 5
- **Clear dimension confirmation** when switching data sources
- **All services refreshed** when data source changes

Try restarting the app and switching data sources - you should see much cleaner output with fewer instances and clear confirmation of the data source switch!

---

_**User (2025-07-27 13:43:35Z)**_

alrightm we ar down to 2, but selecting the data works now

---

_**Agent (2025-07-27 13:43:35Z)**_

Excellent! That's great progress! 🎉

## **What We Fixed:**

### **✅ Reduced Server Instances**
- **Before**: 5 instances (causing data loading 5 times)
- **After**: 2 instances (much cleaner)

### **✅ Data Source Switching Works**
- **Original Data**: `bowling_ergebnisse.csv` (40,500 rows)
- **Real Data**: `bowling_ergebnisse_real.csv` (450 rows)
- **Clear confirmation**: Shows table dimensions when switching

### **✅ All Services Refreshed**
- **Server instances** get new data adapters
- **LeagueService** gets new data adapter
- **All services** use the updated data source

## **What You Should See Now:**

When you switch data sources in the navbar, you should see:
```
✅ DATA SOURCE SWITCHED: bowling_ergebnisse_real.csv
�� Table dimensions: 450 rows × 17 columns
📋 Columns: ['Season', 'Week', 'Date', 'League', ...]
DEBUG: DataManager refreshing 2 server instances
DEBUG: Server refreshing data adapter
DEBUG: Server data adapter refreshed
DEBUG: LeagueService refreshing data adapter
DEBUG: LeagueService data adapter refreshed
```

And when you switch back:
```
✅ DATA SOURCE SWITCHED: bowling_ergebnisse.csv
�� Table dimensions: 40500 rows × 17 columns
📋 Columns: ['Season', 'Week', 'Date', 'League', ...]
```

## **Next Steps:**

Now that the data source switching is working, you should be able to:
1. **Switch between datasets** and see different data in the app
2. **Test the real data** (450 rows) vs original data (40,500 rows)
3. **Verify that the extracted Excel data** is being used correctly

The system is now properly set up to handle multiple data sources! 🚀

---

only fill if player participated in this position\n                for game in games:\n                    game_data = player_rows[player_rows[Columns.round_number] == game]\n                    \n                    if not game_data.empty:\n                        row.append(int(game_data[Columns.score].iloc[0]))\n                        row.append(round(float(game_data[Columns.points].iloc[0]), 1))\n                    else:\n                        row.append(\"\")\n                        row.append(\"\")\n                \n                # Calculate totals for this player-position combination\n                total_points = int(player_rows[Columns.points].sum()) if not player_rows.empty else 0\n                total_score = int(player_rows[Columns.score].sum()) if not player_rows.empty else 0\n                average = round(float(player_rows[Columns.score].mean()), 1) if not player_rows.empty and len(player_rows) \u003e 0 else 0\n                \n                row.extend([total_points, total_score, average])",

        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n    ...\n    def get_team_week_details_table_data(self, league: str, season: str, team: str, week: int) -\u003e TableData:\n\n            ...\n\n            for _, row in player_data.iterrows():\n                player_id = row[Columns.player_id] if not pd.isnull(row[Columns.player_id]) else row[Columns.player_name]\n                player_name = row[Columns.player_name]\n                # Create a unique identifier combining ID and name\n                identifier = f\"{player_id}_{player_name}\"\n                if identifier not in [p['identifier'] for p in player_identifiers]:\n                    player_identifiers.append({\n                        'identifier': identifier,\n                        'player_id': player_id,\n                        'player_name': player_name\n                    })\n            \n            # Create player-position combinations\n            player_position_combinations = []\n            for player_info in player_identifiers:\n                # Find all rows for this player\n                player_rows = player_data[\n                    (player_data[Columns.player_id] == player_info['player_id']) | \n                    (player_data[Columns.player_name] == player_info['player_name'])\n                ]\n                \n                # Get all positions this player played\n                positions = sorted(player_rows[Columns.position].dropna().unique())\n                \n                for position in positions:\n                    player_position_combinations.append({\n                        'player_info': player_info,\n                        'position': position,\n                        'player_rows': player_rows[player_rows[Columns.position] == position]\n                    })\n            \n            # Sort by position first, then by player name\n            player_position_combinations.sort(key=lambda x: (x['position'], x['player_info']['player_name']))\n            \n            # Group by position for merging\n            position_groups = {}",

        "originalContents": "\n            for _, row in player_data.iterrows():\n                player_id = row[Columns.player_id] if not pd.isnull(row[Columns.player_id]) else row[Columns.player_name]\n                player_name = row[Columns.player_name]\n                # Create a unique identifier combining ID and name\n                identifier = f\"{player_id}_{player_name}\"\n                if identifier not in [p['identifier'] for p in player_identifiers]:\n                    player_identifiers.append({\n                        'identifier': identifier,\n                        'player_id': player_id,\n                        'player_name': player_name\n                    })\n            \n            # Create player-position combinations\n            player_position_combinations = []\n            for player_info in player_identifiers:\n                # Find all rows for this player\n                player_rows = player_data[\n                    (player_data[Columns.player_id] == player_info['player_id']) | \n                    (player_data[Columns.player_name] == player_info['player_name'])\n                ]\n                \n                # Get all positions this player played\n                positions = sorted(player_rows[Columns.position].dropna().unique())\n                \n                for position in positions:\n                    player_position_combinations.append({\n                        'player_info': player_info,\n                        'position': position,\n                        'player_rows': player_rows[player_rows[Columns.position] == position]\n                    })\n            \n            # Sort by position first, then by player name\n            player_position_combinations.sort(key=lambda x: (x['position'], x['player_info']['player_name']))\n            \n            # Group by position for merging\n            position_groups = {}",

Get player's position\r\n                'Name': player_name\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            # Add each game's scores and points\r\n            for game_idx, (_, game) in enumerate(player_games.iterrows(), 1):\r\n                player_row[f'Score_{game_idx}'] = int(game[Columns.score])\r\n                player_row[f'Points_{game_idx}'] = float(round(game[Columns.points], 1))\r\n                \r\n            # Add totals\r\n\r\n            player_row.update({\r\n                'Points_Total': float(round(player_games[Columns.points].sum(), 1)),\r\n                'Score_Total': int(player_games[Columns.score].sum()),\r\n                'Score_Average': float(round(player_games[Columns.score].mean(), 2)) if not np.isnan(player_games[Columns.score].mean()) else 0\r\n            })\r\n            \r\n            rows.append(player_row)\r\n        \r\n        #         "contents": "class LeagueService:\n    ...\n    def get_team_week_details_table_data(self, league: str, season: str, team: str, week: int) -\u003e TableData:\n\n                    \n                    ...\n\n                    \n                    # Add metadata for styling\n                    row_metadata.append({\n                        'rowType': 'player',\n                        'styling': {},\n                        'position': int(position),\n                        'isFirstInPosition': False\n                    })\n                \n                data.append(row)\n            \n            # Add team total row\n            if not team_data.empty:\n                # Start with position and name\n                team_row = [\"Team\", team]\n                \n                # Add game data for team\n                for game in games:\n                    game_data = team_data[team_data[Columns.round_number] == game]\n                    if not game_data.empty:\n                        team_row.append(int(game_data[Columns.score].iloc[0]))\n                        team_row.append(round(float(game_data[Columns.points].iloc[0]), 1))\n                    else:\n                        team_row.append(0)\n                        team_row.append(0)\n                \n                # Calculate team totals\n                team_row.append(int(team_data[Columns.points].sum()))\n                team_row.append(int(team_data[Columns.score].sum()))\n                team_row.append(round(float(player_data[Columns.score].mean()), 1) if len(player_data) \u003e 0 else 0)\n                \n                # Add metadata for styling\n                row_metadata.append({\n                    'rowType': 'team',\n                    'styling': {\n                        'fontWeight': 'bold',\n                        'borderTop': '2px solid #000000'\n                    }\n                })\n                \n                data.append(team_row)",

        "contents": "class Server:\r\n    # fetches basic dataframes from data adapter\r\n    # converts basic dataframes to aggregated dataframes\r\n    # forwards aggregated dataframes to app\n    ...\n    def get_team_week_details(self, league_name: str, season: str, team_name: str, week: int, debug_output: bool=False) -\u003e dict:\n\r\n\r\n        ...\n\r\n\r\n        team_opponent_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name_opponent: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: True\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        columns_team = [Columns.score, Columns.points, Columns.team_name, Columns.team_name_opponent, Columns.week]\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n        team_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            columns=columns_team,\r\n            filters_eq=team_filters\r\n        )\r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"team_data: \\n\" + str(team_data))\r\n        \r\n\r\n        team_opponent_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            columns=columns_team,\r\n            filters_eq=team_opponent_filters\r\n        )\r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"team_opponent_data: \\n\" + str(team_opponent_data))\r\n\r\n        # Get number of games\r\n        n_games = len(player_data[Columns.team_name_opponent].unique())\r\n        \r\n        # Prepare player rows\r\n        rows = []\r\n        # Group by player to get their games\r\n        player_groups = player_data.groupby(Columns.player_name)\r\n        player_groups = sorted(player_groups, key=lambda x: x[1][Columns.position].iloc[0])",

        "contents": "class Server:\r\n    # fetches basic dataframes from data adapter\r\n    # converts basic dataframes to aggregated dataframes\r\n    # forwards aggregated dataframes to app\n    ...\n    def get_team_week_details(self, league_name: str, season: str, team_name: str, week: int, debug_output: bool=False) -\u003e dict:\n\r\n\r\n        ...\n\r\n\r\n        # Add team totals\r\n        team_row.update({\r\n            'Points_Total': float(round(team_data[Columns.points].sum(), 1)),\r\n            'Score_Total': int(team_data[Columns.score].sum()),\r\n            'Score_Average': float(round(player_data[Columns.score].mean(), 2)) if not np.isnan(team_data[Columns.score].mean()) else 0\r\n        })\r\n\r\n        # Add opponent totals\r\n        opponent_row.update({\r\n            'Points_Total': float(round(team_opponent_data[Columns.points].sum(), 1)),\r\n            'Score_Total': int(team_opponent_data[Columns.score].sum()),\r\n            'Score_Average': float(round(team_opponent_data[Columns.score].mean(), 2)) if not np.isnan(team_opponent_data[Columns.score].mean()) else 0\r\n        })\r\n        \r\n        rows.append(team_row)\r\n        rows.append(opponent_row)\r\n\r\n        # Prepare header groups for the table\r\n        header_groups = [\r\n            { 'title': 'Player', 'colspan': 2 }\r\n        ]\r\n        \r\n        # Add game columns\r\n        for game_idx in range(1, n_games + 1):\r\n            header_groups.append({ \r\n                'title': f'Game {game_idx}', \r\n                'colspan': 2 \r\n            })\r\n        \r\n        #         "originalContents": "\n            \n            # Add game column groups with opponent team names\n            for game in games:\n                opponent_name = game_to_opponent.get(game, f\"Game {game}\")\n                columns.append(\n                    ColumnGroup(\n                        title=opponent_name,\n                        columns=[\n                            Column(title=\"Score\", field=f\"game{game}_score\", width=\"80px\", align=\"center\"),\n                            Column(title=\"Pts\", field=f\"game{game}_points\", width=\"60px\", align=\"center\")\n                        ]\n                    )\n                )\n            \n            # Add totals column group\n            columns.append(\n                ColumnGroup(\n                    title=\"Total\",\n                    style={\"backgroundColor\": \"#e9ecef\"},\n                    header_style={\"fontWeight\": \"bold\"},\n                    columns=[\n                        Column(title=\"Points\", field=\"total_points\", width=\"80px\", align=\"center\"),\n                        Column(title=\"Score\", field=\"total_score\", width=\"80px\", align=\"center\"),\n                        Column(title=\"Avg\", field=\"average\", width=\"80px\", align=\"center\")\n                    ]\n                )\n            )\n            \n            # Prepare data rows with merged position cells\n            data = []\n            row_metadata = []\n            \n            # Get all unique players who played in this event (same logic as New view)\n            player_identifiers = []",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n    ...\n    def get_team_week_head_to_head_table_data(self, league: str, season: str, team: str, week: int, view_mode: str = 'own_team') -\u003e TableData:\n\n            \n            ...\n\n            \n            # --- Build data rows ---\n            if view_mode == 'own_team':\n                # Build main data rows (one per round)\n                data = []\n                for rnd in all_rounds:\n                    # Get opponent team name for this round (from own or opponent data)\n                    if rnd in team_data[Columns.round_number].values:\n                        opponent_team = team_data[team_data[Columns.round_number] == rnd][Columns.team_name_opponent].iloc[0]\n                    elif rnd in opponent_data[Columns.round_number].values:\n                        opponent_team = opponent_data[opponent_data[Columns.round_number] == rnd][Columns.team_name].iloc[0]\n                    else:\n                        opponent_team = \"\"\n                    row = [int(rnd), opponent_team]\n                    # For each player, always output pos, score, points (blank if not played)\n                    for player in own_players:\n                        if rnd in own_part_map[player]:\n                            row_data = own_part_map[player][rnd]\n                            row.append(int(row_data[Columns.position] + 1) if not pd.isnull(row_data[Columns.position]) else \"\")\n                            row.append(int(row_data[Columns.score]) if not pd.isnull(row_data[Columns.score]) else \"\")\n                            row.append(float(row_data[Columns.points]) if not pd.isnull(row_data[Columns.points]) else \"\")\n                        else:\n                            row.extend([\"\", \"\", \"\"])\n                    # Team totals for this round (if available)\n                    team_totals = team_data[(team_data[Columns.round_number] == rnd) \u0026 (team_data[Columns.computed_data] == True)]\n                    if not team_totals.empty:\n                        row.append(int(team_totals[Columns.score].iloc[0]))\n                        row.append(int(team_totals[Columns.points].iloc[0]))\n                    else:\n                        row.extend([\"\", \"\"])\n                    data.append(row)",

        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n    ...\n    def get_team_week_details_table_data(self, league: str, season: str, team: str, week: int) -\u003e TableData:\n\n            \n            ...\n\n            \n            opponent_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=opponent_filters)\n            \n            # Get individual opponent data to calculate average based on individual results\n            opponent_individual_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.team_name_opponent: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}  # Individual data, not computed\n            }\n            \n            opponent_individual_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=opponent_individual_filters)\n            \n            # Get unique games (round_number) for this team and map to opponent names\n            games = sorted(player_data[Columns.round_number].unique())\n            \n            # Create a mapping of round_number to opponent team name\n            game_to_opponent = {}\n            for game in games:\n                game_data = player_data[player_data[Columns.round_number] == game]\n                if not game_data.empty:\n                    opponent_name = game_data[Columns.team_name_opponent].iloc[0]\n                    game_to_opponent[game] = opponent_name\n            \n            # Create column groups\n            columns = [\n                ColumnGroup(\n                    title=\"Player\",\n                    frozen=\"left\",\n                    style={\"backgroundColor\": \"#f8f9fa\"},\n                    columns=[\n                        Column(title=\"Pos\", field=\"position\", width=\"50px\", align=\"center\"),\n                        Column(title=\"Name\", field=\"name\", width=\"200px\", align=\"left\")\n                    ]\n                )\n            ]",

        "originalContents": "\n            \n            opponent_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=opponent_filters)\n            \n            # Get individual opponent data to calculate average based on individual results\n            opponent_individual_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.team_name_opponent: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}  # Individual data, not computed\n            }\n            \n            opponent_individual_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=opponent_individual_filters)\n            \n            # Get unique games (round_number) for this team and map to opponent names\n            games = sorted(player_data[Columns.round_number].unique())\n            \n            # Create a mapping of round_number to opponent team name\n            game_to_opponent = {}\n            for game in games:\n                game_data = player_data[player_data[Columns.round_number] == game]\n                if not game_data.empty:\n                    opponent_name = game_data[Columns.team_name_opponent].iloc[0]\n                    game_to_opponent[game] = opponent_name\n            \n            # Create column groups\n            columns = [\n                ColumnGroup(\n                    title=\"Player\",\n                    frozen=\"left\",\n                    style={\"backgroundColor\": \"#f8f9fa\"},\n                    columns=[\n                        Column(title=\"Pos\", field=\"position\", width=\"50px\", align=\"center\"),\n                        Column(title=\"Name\", field=\"name\", width=\"200px\", align=\"left\")\n                    ]\n                )\n            ]",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n    ...\n    def get_team_individual_scores_table(self, league: str, season: str, team: str, week: int) -\u003e TableData:\n\n            \n            ...\n\n            \n            # For each player, get their data for this round\n            for player_info in player_identifiers:\n                # Find all rows for this player in this round\n                pdata = round_data[\n                    (round_data[Columns.player_id] == player_info['player_id']) | \n                    (round_data[Columns.player_name] == player_info['player_name'])\n                ]\n                \n                if not pdata.empty:\n                    # If player played multiple positions, aggregate the data\n                    total_score = int(pdata[Columns.score].sum()) if not pdata.empty else \"\"\n                    total_points = float(pdata[Columns.points].sum()) if not pdata.empty else \"\"\n                    \n                    # For position, show all positions played (e.g., \"0,1\" if played both positions)\n                    positions = sorted(pdata[Columns.position].dropna().unique())\n                    pos_str = \",\".join([str(int(pos + 1)) for pos in positions]) if len(positions) \u003e 0 else \"\"\n                    \n                    row.extend([total_score, total_points, pos_str])\n                else:\n                    row.extend([\"\", \"\", \"\"])\n                    data.append(row)\n            row_metadata.append({'rowType': 'match', 'styling': {}})\n\n        # Add summary row\n        summary_row = [\"Total\", 0, 0.0, 0.0]\n        for player_info in player_identifiers:\n            # Find all rows for this player in the entire event\n            player_rows = player_data[\n                (player_data[Columns.player_id] == player_info['player_id']) | \n                (player_data[Columns.player_name] == player_info['player_name'])\n            ]\n            total_score = int(player_rows[Columns.score].sum()) if not player_rows.empty else 0\n            total_points = float(player_rows[Columns.points].sum()) if not player_rows.empty else 0.0\n            summary_row.extend([total_score, total_points, \"\"])",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "\n            \n            # For each player, get their data for this round\n            for player_info in player_identifiers:\n                # Find all rows for this player in this round\n                pdata = round_data[\n                    (round_data[Columns.player_id] == player_info['player_id']) | \n                    (round_data[Columns.player_name] == player_info['player_name'])\n                ]\n                \n                if not pdata.empty:\n                    # If player played multiple positions, aggregate the data\n                    total_score = int(pdata[Columns.score].sum()) if not pdata.empty else \"\"\n                    total_points = float(pdata[Columns.points].sum()) if not pdata.empty else \"\"\n                    \n                    # For position, show all positions played (e.g., \"0,1\" if played both positions)\n                    positions = sorted(pdata[Columns.position].dropna().unique())\n                    pos_str = \",\".join([str(int(pos + 1)) for pos in positions]) if len(positions) \u003e 0 else \"\"\n                    \n                    row.extend([total_score, total_points, pos_str])\n                else:\n                    row.extend([\"\", \"\", \"\"])\n                    data.append(row)\n            row_metadata.append({'rowType': 'match', 'styling': {}})\n\n        # Add summary row\n        summary_row = [\"Total\", 0, 0.0, 0.0]\n        for player_info in player_identifiers:\n            # Find all rows for this player in the entire event\n            player_rows = player_data[\n                (player_data[Columns.player_id] == player_info['player_id']) | \n                (player_data[Columns.player_name] == player_info['player_name'])\n            ]\n            total_score = int(player_rows[Columns.score].sum()) if not player_rows.empty else 0\n            total_points = float(player_rows[Columns.points].sum()) if not player_rows.empty else 0.0\n            summary_row.extend([total_score, total_points, \"\"])",

        "originalContents": "\n\n    def get_team_week_details_table_data(self, league: str, season: str, team: str, week: int) -\u003e TableData:\n        \"\"\"Get team week details as a TableData object for rendering\"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Get individual player results for the team\n            player_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.team_name: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }\n            \n            player_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=player_filters)\n            \n            if player_data.empty:\n                return TableData(\n                    columns=[],\n                    data=[],\n                    title=f\"No data available for {team} - Week {week}\"\n                )\n            \n            # Get team totals (computed data)\n            team_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.team_name: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }\n            \n            team_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=team_filters)\n            \n            # Get opponent team totals\n            opponent_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.team_name_opponent: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class Server:\r\n    # fetches basic dataframes from data adapter\r\n    # converts basic dataframes to aggregated dataframes\r\n    # forwards aggregated dataframes to app\n\r\n\r\n    ...\n\r\n\r\n    def get_team_week_details(self, league_name: str, season: str, team_name: str, week: int, debug_output: bool=False) -\u003e dict:\r\n        \"\"\"Get detailed team results for a specific week including individual and team stats\"\"\"\r\n        \r\n        # Get individual player results\r\n        player_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: False\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        player_opponent_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name_opponent: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: False\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        player_columns = [\r\n            Columns.player_name,\r\n            Columns.score,\r\n            Columns.points,\r\n            Columns.team_name_opponent,\r\n            Columns.match_number,\r\n            Columns.position, \r\n            Columns.week\r\n        ]\r\n        \r\n        player_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            columns=player_columns,\r\n            filters_eq=player_filters\r\n        )\r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"player_data: \\n\" + str(player_data))\r\n        \r\n        player_opponent_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            columns=player_columns,\r\n            filters_eq=player_opponent_filters\r\n        )\r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"player_opponent_data: \\n\" + str(player_opponent_data))\r\n\r\n        # Get team results\r\n        team_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: True\r\n        }",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n    def get_team_week_details(self, league_name: str, season: str, team_name: str, week: int, debug_output: bool=False) -\u003e dict:\r\n        \"\"\"Get detailed team results for a specific week including individual and team stats\"\"\"\r\n        \r\n        # Get individual player results\r\n        player_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: False\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        player_opponent_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name_opponent: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: False\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        player_columns = [\r\n            Columns.player_name,\r\n            Columns.score,\r\n            Columns.points,\r\n            Columns.team_name_opponent,\r\n            Columns.match_number,\r\n            Columns.position, \r\n            Columns.week\r\n        ]\r\n        \r\n        player_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            columns=player_columns,\r\n            filters_eq=player_filters\r\n        )\r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"player_data: \\n\" + str(player_data))\r\n        \r\n        player_opponent_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            columns=player_columns,\r\n            filters_eq=player_opponent_filters\r\n        )\r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"player_opponent_data: \\n\" + str(player_opponent_data))\r\n\r\n        # Get team results\r\n        team_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: True\r\n        }",

_**Agent**_

Now I can see the issue! The problem is on line 730: `data.append(row)` is outside the loop that processes additional players in the same position. This means that when there's only one player in a position, the row gets added twice:

1. First time: Inside the main loop (around line 690)
2. Second time: Outside the loop (line 730)

Let me fix this by moving the `data.append(row)` inside the loop for additional players:


_**Agent**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="search_replace">

<details><summary>Tool use: **search_replace** • Edit file: app/services/league_service.py</summary>

**Chunk 1**
Lines added: 2, lines removed: 2

```diff
@@ -732,6 +732,6 @@
                          'isFirstInPosition': False
                      })
-                 
-                 data.append(row)
+                     
+                     data.append(row)
              
              # Add team total row
```

</details>

</tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="search_replace"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **search_replace**</summary>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent**_

Perfect! Now the `data.append(row)` is inside the loop for additional players, so it will only be called when there are actually additional players in the same position. This should fix the duplication issue.

## **What Was Fixed:**

### **✅ Duplicate Row Issue**
- **Problem**: When only one player played at a position, the row was being added twice
- **Root Cause**: `data.append(row)` was outside the loop that processes additional players
- **Fix**: Moved `data.append(row)` inside the loop so it only executes when there are actually additional players

### **✅ Expected Result:**
Now when you view the Team Week Details Table:
- **Single player positions**: Only one row per player (no duplicates)
- **Multiple players per position**: Properly merged rows with correct formatting
- **Lower rows**: Team totals and opponent data will align correctly

The table should now display cleanly without the formatting issues caused by duplicate rows! 🎯

---

        "contents": "function updateTableTeamDetails() {\r\n    const selectedSeason = document.querySelector('input[name=\"season\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedLeague = document.querySelector('input[name=\"league\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedWeek = document.querySelector('input[name=\"week\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedTeam = document.querySelector('input[name=\"team\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    const selectedView = document.querySelector('input[name=\"teamView\"]:checked')?.value;\r\n    let endpoint = '/league/get_team_week_details_table';\r\n    let viewMode = undefined;\r\n    if (selectedView === 'new') {\r\n        endpoint = '/league/get_team_individual_scores_table';\r\n    } else if (selectedView === 'newFull') {\r\n        endpoint = '/league/get_team_week_head_to_head_table';\r\n        viewMode = 'full';\r\n    }\r\n    if (!selectedSeason || !selectedLeague || !selectedWeek || !selectedTeam) {\r\n        return;\r\n    }\r\n    // Update header\r\n    const matchDayText = window.translations ? (window.translations['match_day_label'] || 'Match Day') : 'Match Day';\r\n    document.getElementById('teamDetailsMetadata').innerHTML = &#96;${selectedTeam} - ${matchDayText} ${selectedWeek}&#96;;\r\n    // Use fetchAndRenderTable for consistent table rendering\r\n    const params = {\r\n        season: selectedSeason,\r\n        league: selectedLeague,\r\n        week: selectedWeek,\r\n        team: selectedTeam,\r\n        options: {\r\n            disablePositionCircle: true,\r\n            mergeCells: false,\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true\r\n        }\r\n    };\r\n    if (viewMode) {\r\n        params.view_mode = viewMode;\r\n    }\r\n    fetchAndRenderTable('teamTableWeekDetails', endpoint, params);\r\n}\r\n\r\n",

(window.translations['match_day_label'] || 'Match Day') : 'Match Day';\r\n    document.getElementById('teamDetailsMetadata').innerHTML = &#96;${selectedTeam} - ${matchDayText} ${selectedWeek}&#96;;\r\n    // Use fetchAndRenderTable for consistent table rendering\r\n    const params = {\r\n        season: selectedSeason,\r\n        league: selectedLeague,\r\n        week: selectedWeek,\r\n        team: selectedTeam,\r\n        options: {\r\n            disablePositionCircle: true,\r\n            mergeCells: false,\r\n            enableSpecialRowStyling: true\r\n        }\r\n    };\r\n    if (viewMode) {\r\n        params.view_mode = viewMode;\r\n    }\r\n    fetchAndRenderTable('teamTableWeekDetails', endpoint, params);\r\n}\r\n\r\n",

        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n\n\n    ...\n\n\n    def get_team_week_details_table_data(self, league: str, season: str, team: str, week: int) -\u003e TableData:\n        \"\"\"Get team week details as a TableData object for rendering\"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Get individual player results for the team\n            player_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.team_name: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }\n            \n            player_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=player_filters)\n            \n            if player_data.empty:\n                return TableData(\n                    columns=[],\n                    data=[],\n                    title=f\"No data available for {team} - Week {week}\"\n                )\n            \n            # Get team totals (computed data)\n            team_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.team_name: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }\n            \n            team_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=team_filters)\n            \n            # Get opponent team totals\n            opponent_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.team_name_opponent: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }",

        "originalContents": "\n\n    def get_team_week_details_table_data(self, league: str, season: str, team: str, week: int) -\u003e TableData:\n        \"\"\"Get team week details as a TableData object for rendering\"\"\"\n        try:\n            # Get individual player results for the team\n            player_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.team_name: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': False, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }\n            \n            player_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=player_filters)\n            \n            if player_data.empty:\n                return TableData(\n                    columns=[],\n                    data=[],\n                    title=f\"No data available for {team} - Week {week}\"\n                )\n            \n            # Get team totals (computed data)\n            team_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.team_name: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }\n            \n            team_data = self.adapter.get_filtered_data(filters=team_filters)\n            \n            # Get opponent team totals\n            opponent_filters = {\n                Columns.league_name: {'value': league, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.season: {'value': season, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.team_name_opponent: {'value': team, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.week: {'value': week, 'operator': 'eq'},\n                Columns.computed_data: {'value': True, 'operator': 'eq'}\n            }",

        "contents": "class Server:\r\n    # fetches basic dataframes from data adapter\r\n    # converts basic dataframes to aggregated dataframes\r\n    # forwards aggregated dataframes to app\n\r\n\r\n    ...\n\r\n\r\n    def get_team_week_details(self, league_name: str, season: str, team_name: str, week: int, debug_output: bool=False) -\u003e dict:\r\n        \"\"\"Get detailed team results for a specific week including individual and team stats\"\"\"\r\n        \r\n        # Get individual player results\r\n        player_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: False\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        player_opponent_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name_opponent: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: False\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        player_columns = [\r\n            Columns.player_name,\r\n            Columns.score,\r\n            Columns.points,\r\n            Columns.team_name_opponent,\r\n            Columns.match_number,\r\n            Columns.position, \r\n            Columns.week\r\n        ]\r\n        \r\n        player_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            columns=player_columns,\r\n            filters_eq=player_filters\r\n        )\r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"player_data: \\n\" + str(player_data))\r\n        \r\n        player_opponent_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            columns=player_columns,\r\n            filters_eq=player_opponent_filters\r\n        )\r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"player_opponent_data: \\n\" + str(player_opponent_data))\r\n\r\n        #         "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n    def get_team_week_details(self, league_name: str, season: str, team_name: str, week: int, debug_output: bool=False) -\u003e dict:\r\n        \"\"\"Get detailed team results for a specific week including individual and team stats\"\"\"\r\n        \r\n        # Get individual player results\r\n        player_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: False\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        player_opponent_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name_opponent: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: False\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        player_columns = [\r\n            Columns.player_name,\r\n            Columns.score,\r\n            Columns.points,\r\n            Columns.team_name_opponent,\r\n            Columns.match_number,\r\n            Columns.position, \r\n            Columns.week\r\n        ]\r\n        \r\n        player_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            columns=player_columns,\r\n            filters_eq=player_filters\r\n        )\r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"player_data: \\n\" + str(player_data))\r\n        \r\n        player_opponent_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            columns=player_columns,\r\n            filters_eq=player_opponent_filters\r\n        )\r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"player_opponent_data: \\n\" + str(player_opponent_data))\r\n\r\n        # Get team results\r\n        team_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: True\r\n        }",

        "contents": "class Server:\r\n    # fetches basic dataframes from data adapter\r\n    # converts basic dataframes to aggregated dataframes\r\n    # forwards aggregated dataframes to app\n    ...\n    def get_team_week_details(self, league_name: str, season: str, team_name: str, week: int, debug_output: bool=False) -\u003e dict:\n\r\n        \r\n        ...\n\r\n        \r\n        for player_name, player_games in player_groups:\r\n            player_row = {\r\n                'type': 'player',\r\n                'Position': int(player_games[Columns.position].iloc[0]),  # Get player's position\r\n                'Name': player_name\r\n            }\r\n            \r\n            # Add each game's scores and points\r\n            for game_idx, (_, game) in enumerate(player_games.iterrows(), 1):\r\n                player_row[f'Score_{game_idx}'] = int(game[Columns.score])\r\n                player_row[f'Points_{game_idx}'] = float(round(game[Columns.points], 1))\r\n                \r\n            # Add totals\r\n\r\n            player_row.update({\r\n                'Points_Total': float(round(player_games[Columns.points].sum(), 1)),\r\n                'Score_Total': int(player_games[Columns.score].sum()),\r\n                'Score_Average': float(round(player_games[Columns.score].mean(), 2)) if not np.isnan(player_games[Columns.score].mean()) else 0\r\n            })\r\n            \r\n            rows.append(player_row)\r\n        \r\n        # Add team row\r\n        team_row = {\r\n            'type': 'team',\r\n            'Position': '-',\r\n            'Name': team_name\r\n        }\r\n\r\n        opponent_row = {\r\n            'type': 'opponent',\r\n            'Position': '-',\r\n            'Name': 'Opponents'\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        # Add team scores and points for each game\r\n        for game_idx, (_, game) in enumerate(team_data.iterrows(), 1):\r\n            team_row[f'Score_{game_idx}'] = int(game[Columns.score])\r\n            team_row[f'Points_{game_idx}'] = float(round(game[Columns.points], 1))\r\n        \r\n\r\n        # Add opponent scores and points for each game\r\n        for game_idx, (_, game) in enumerate(team_opponent_data.iterrows(), 1):\r\n            opponent_row[f'Score_{game_idx}'] = int(game[Columns.score])\r\n            opponent_row[f'Points_{game_idx}'] = float(round(game[Columns.points], 1))",

        "contents": "class Server:\r\n    # fetches basic dataframes from data adapter\r\n    # converts basic dataframes to aggregated dataframes\r\n    # forwards aggregated dataframes to app\n    ...\n    def get_team_week_details(self, league_name: str, season: str, team_name: str, week: int, debug_output: bool=False) -\u003e dict:\n\r\n\r\n        ...\n\r\n\r\n        team_opponent_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name_opponent: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: True\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        columns_team = [Columns.score, Columns.points, Columns.team_name, Columns.team_name_opponent, Columns.week]\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n        team_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            columns=columns_team,\r\n            filters_eq=team_filters\r\n        )\r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"team_data: \\n\" + str(team_data))\r\n        \r\n\r\n        team_opponent_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            columns=columns_team,\r\n            filters_eq=team_opponent_filters\r\n        )\r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"team_opponent_data: \\n\" + str(team_opponent_data))\r\n\r\n        # Get number of games\r\n        n_games = len(player_data[Columns.team_name_opponent].unique())\r\n        \r\n        # Prepare player rows\r\n        rows = []\r\n        # Group by player to get their games\r\n        player_groups = player_data.groupby(Columns.player_name)\r\n        player_groups = sorted(player_groups, key=lambda x: x[1][Columns.position].iloc[0])",

        "originalContents": "\r\n\r\n        team_opponent_filters = {\r\n            Columns.league_name: league_name,\r\n            Columns.season: season,\r\n            Columns.team_name_opponent: team_name,\r\n            Columns.week: week,\r\n            Columns.computed_data: True\r\n        }\r\n        \r\n        columns_team = [Columns.score, Columns.points, Columns.team_name, Columns.team_name_opponent, Columns.week]\r\n\r\n\r\n\r\n        team_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            columns=columns_team,\r\n            filters_eq=team_filters\r\n        )\r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"team_data: \\n\" + str(team_data))\r\n        \r\n\r\n        team_opponent_data = self.data_adapter.get_filtered_data(\r\n            columns=columns_team,\r\n            filters_eq=team_opponent_filters\r\n        )\r\n        if debug_output:\r\n            print(\"team_opponent_data: \\n\" + str(team_opponent_data))\r\n\r\n        # Get number of games\r\n        n_games = len(player_data[Columns.team_name_opponent].unique())\r\n        \r\n        # Prepare player rows\r\n        rows = []\r\n        # Group by player to get their games\r\n        player_groups = player_data.groupby(Columns.player_name)\r\n        player_groups = sorted(player_groups, key=lambda x: x[1][Columns.position].iloc[0])",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n    ...\n    def get_team_week_details_table_data(self, league: str, season: str, team: str, week: int) -\u003e TableData:\n\n            ...\n\n            for combo in player_position_combinations:\n                position = combo['position']\n                if position not in position_groups:\n                    position_groups[position] = []\n                position_groups[position].append(combo)\n            \n            # Create merged data structure\n            for position in sorted(position_groups.keys()):\n                position_combos = position_groups[position]\n                \n                # Add first row of this position group with position number\n                first_combo = position_combos[0]\n                player_info = first_combo['player_info']\n                player_rows = first_combo['player_rows']\n                \n                # Start with position and name\n                row = [int(position + 1), player_info['player_name']]\n                \n                # Add game data - only fill if player participated in this position\n                for game in games:\n                    game_data = player_rows[player_rows[Columns.round_number] == game]\n                    \n                    if not game_data.empty:\n                        row.append(int(game_data[Columns.score].iloc[0]))\n                        row.append(round(float(game_data[Columns.points].iloc[0]), 1))\n                    else:\n                        row.append(\"\")\n                        row.append(\"\")\n                \n                # Calculate totals for this player-position combination\n                total_points = int(player_rows[Columns.points].sum()) if not player_rows.empty else 0\n                total_score = int(player_rows[Columns.score].sum()) if not player_rows.empty else 0\n                average = round(float(player_rows[Columns.score].mean()), 1) if not player_rows.empty and len(player_rows) \u003e 0 else 0\n                \n                row.extend([total_points, total_score, average])",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "class LeagueService:\n    ...\n    def get_team_week_head_to_head_table_data(self, league: str, season: str, team: str, week: int, view_mode: str = 'own_team') -\u003e TableData:\n\n            \n            ...\n\n            \n            # --- Build data rows ---\n            if view_mode == 'own_team':\n                # Build main data rows (one per round)\n                data = []\n                for rnd in all_rounds:\n                    # Get opponent team name for this round (from own or opponent data)\n                    if rnd in team_data[Columns.round_number].values:\n                        opponent_team = team_data[team_data[Columns.round_number] == rnd][Columns.team_name_opponent].iloc[0]\n                    elif rnd in opponent_data[Columns.round_number].values:\n                        opponent_team = opponent_data[opponent_data[Columns.round_number] == rnd][Columns.team_name].iloc[0]\n                    else:\n                        opponent_team = \"\"\n                    row = [int(rnd), opponent_team]\n                    # For each player, always output pos, score, points (blank if not played)\n                    for player in own_players:\n                        if rnd in own_part_map[player]:\n                            row_data = own_part_map[player][rnd]\n                            row.append(int(row_data[Columns.position] + 1) if not pd.isnull(row_data[Columns.position]) else \"\")\n                            row.append(int(row_data[Columns.score]) if not pd.isnull(row_data[Columns.score]) else \"\")\n                            row.append(float(row_data[Columns.points]) if not pd.isnull(row_data[Columns.points]) else \"\")\n                        else:\n                            row.extend([\"\", \"\", \"\"])\n                    # Team totals for this round (if available)\n                    team_totals = team_data[(team_data[Columns.round_number] == rnd) \u0026 (team_data[Columns.computed_data] == True)]\n                    if not team_totals.empty:\n                        row.append(int(team_totals[Columns.score].iloc[0]))\n                        row.append(int(team_totals[Columns.points].iloc[0]))\n                    else:\n                        row.extend([\"\", \"\"])\n                    data.append(row)",
